entries.description: While wearing this hat, you can use an action to cast the {@spell Disguise Self|PHB} spell from it at will. The spell ends if the hat is removed.

entries.name: HEADBAND OF INTELLECT
effects: Your Intelligence score is 19 while you wear this headband.
This effect has no impact if your Intelligence is already 19 or higher.

entries.name: Benefits
entries: You can use an action to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18), using one of the helm's gems of the specified type as a component: {@item HELM OF BRILLIANCE|DMG} (opal), fireball (fire opal), prismatic spray (diamond), or wall of fire (ruby). The gem is destroyed when the spell is cast and disappears from the helm.

As long as it has at least one diamond, the helm emits dim light in a 30-foot radius when at least one undead is within that area. Any undead that starts its turn in that area takes {@damage 1d6 radiant} damage.
As long as the helm has at least one ruby, you have resistance to fire damage.

As long as the helm has at least one fire opal, you can use an action and speak a command word to cause one weapon you are holding to burst into flames. The flames emit bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. The flames are harmless to you and the weapon. When you hit with an attack using the blazing weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6 fire} damage. The flames last until you use a bonus action to speak the command word again or until you drop or stow the weapon.

entries.name: Special Reaction
entries: Roll a d20 if you are wearing the helm and take fire damage as a result of failing a saving throw against a spell. On a roll of 1, the helm emits beams of light from its remaining gems. Each creature within 60 feet of the helm other than you must succeed on a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw or be struck by a beam, taking radiant damage equal to the number of gems in the helm. The helm and its gems are then destroyed.

entries.name: Helm of Comprehending Languages
entries.description: While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast the {@spell comprehend languages} spell from it at will.

entries.name: Detect Thoughts
entries: While wearing this helm, you can use an action to cast {@spell detect-thoughts|PHB} (save DC 13) from it.

As long as you maintain concentration on the spell, you can use a bonus action to send a telepathic message to a creature you are focused on. The creature can reply using a bonus action to do so while your focus on it continues.

entries.name: Suggestion
entries: While focusing on a creature with detect thoughts, you can use an action to cast {@spell suggestion|PHB} (save DC 13) from the helm on that creature.
Once used, the suggestion property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Helm of Teleportation
entries.description: While wearing it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast the teleport spell from it. The helm regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Heward's Handy Haversack
entries.description: This backpack has a central pouch and two side pouches, each of which is an extradimensional space. Each side pouch can hold up to 20 pounds of material, not exceeding a volume of 2 cubic feet. The large central pouch can hold up to 8 cubic feet or 80 pounds of material. The backpack always weighs 5 pounds, regardless of its contents. Placing an object in the haversack follows the normal rules for interacting with objects. Retrieving an item from the haversack requires you to use an action. When you reach into the haversack for a specific item, the item is always magically on top.
limitations: Overloading the haversack or piercing/tearing it destroys the item, causing its contents to be lost (though an artifact reappears elsewhere).
Turning the haversack inside out spills its contents, requiring it to be righted before use.
A breathing creature placed inside suffocates after 10 minutes.

Placing the haversack in an extradimensional space (e.g., bag of holding) destroys both items and opens a one-way gate to the Astral Plane, pulling creatures within 10 feet through to a random location.

entries.name: Holy Avenger
entries.description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a fiend or an undead with it, that creature takes an extra {@damage 2d10} radiant damage. While you hold the drawn sword, it creates an aura in a 10-foot radius around you. You and all creatures friendly to you in the aura have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects. If you have 17 or more levels in the paladin class, the radius of the aura increases to 30 feet.

entries.name: HORN OF BLASTING
entries.description: You can use an action to speak the horn's command word and then blow the horn, which emits a thunderous blast in a 30-foot cone that is audible 600 feet away. Each creature in the cone must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d6} thunder damage and is deafened for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and isn't deafened. Creatures and objects made of glass or crystal have disadvantage on the saving throw and take {@damage 10d6} thunder damage instead of 5d6. Each use of the horn's magic has a 20 percent chance of causing the horn to explode. The explosion deals {@damage 10d6} fire damage to the blower and destroys the horn.

entries.name: Horn of Valhalla
entries: A wondrous item with rarity determined by its metal composition: silver/brass (rare), bronze (very rare), iron (legendary).
Use an action to blow the horn, summoning warrior spirits from the plane of Ysgard within 60 feet. These spirits use berserker statistics from the Monster Manual.
The summoned berserkers return to Ysgard after 1 hour or when they drop to 0 hit points. The horn cannot be used again for 7 days after its first use.
Four types exist, each with different summoning effects and requirements: {@item Horn of Valhalla|DMG} (silver/brass), {@item Horn of Valhalla|DMG} (bronze), {@item Horn of Valhalla|DMG} (iron).
If the user does not meet the horn's requirement, the berserkers attack them. Meeting the requirement makes them friendly and obedient to commands.

entries.name: Horseshoes of a Zephyr
entries.description: These iron horseshoes come in a set of four. While all four shoes are affixed to the hooves of a horse or similar creature, they allow the creature to move normally while floating 4 inches above the ground. This effect means the creature can cross or stand above nonsolid or unstable surfaces, such as water or lava. The creature leaves no tracks and ignores difficult terrain. In addition, the creature can move at normal speed for up to 12 hours a day without suffering exhaustion from a forced march.

entries.name: Horseshoes of Speed
entries.description: These iron horseshoes come in a set of four. While all four shoes are affixed to the hooves of a horse or similar creature, they increase the creature's walking speed by 30 feet.

entries.name: Rod
entries.description: This flat iron rod has a button on one end. You can use an action to press the button, which causes the rod to become magically fixed in place. Until you or another creature uses an action to push the button again, the rod doesn't move, even if it is defying gravity. The rod can hold up to 8,000 pounds of weight. More weight causes the rod to deactivate and fall. A creature can use an action to make a {@dc 30} Strength check, moving the fixed rod up to {@distance 10 feet} on a success.

entries.name: Instrument of the Bards
entries.description: An exquisite instrument superior to ordinary ones, tied to legendary bard colleges. Requires attunement by a bard.

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: You can use an action to play the instrument and cast one of its spells. Once used for a spell, it cannot be used again until the next dawn.
Spells use your spellcasting ability and spell save DC.

entries.name: Unattuned Use
entries: A creature attempting to play without attunement must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or take {@damage 2d4 psychic} damage.

entries.name: Charmed Targets
entries: When casting a spell that causes charmed targets on a failed save, those targets have disadvantage on their saving throws.

entries.name: Ioun Stone
entries.description: Named after Ioun, a god of knowledge and prophecy revered on some worlds. Many types exist, each a distinct combination of shape and color.

entries.name: Core Function
entries.description: When tossed into the air, the stone orbits your head at a distance of 1d3 feet and confers a benefit. Another creature must use an action to grasp or net the stone (AC 24, DC 24 Dexterity [Acrobatics] check). You can use an action to seize and stow the stone, ending its effect.

entries.name: Stone Properties
entries.description: AC 24, 10 hit points, resistance to all damage. Considered an object being worn while orbiting your head.

entries.name: Absorption (Very Rare)
entries.description: Use reaction to cancel a spell of 4th level or lower cast by a creature you can see and targeting only you. Burns out after canceling 20 levels of spells (turns dull gray). Cannot cancel spells exceeding remaining spell levels.

entries.name: Agility (Very Rare)
entries.description: Dexterity score increases by 2 (max 20) while this deep red sphere orbits your head.

entries.name: Awareness (Rare)
entries.description: Cannot be surprised while this dark blue rhomboid orbits your head.

entries.name: Fortitude (Very Rare)
entries.description: Constitution score increases by 2 (max 20) while this pink rhomboid orbits your head.

entries.name: Greater Absorption (Legendary)
entries.description: Use reaction to cancel a spell of 8th level or lower. Burns out after canceling 50 levels of spells (turns dull gray). Cannot cancel spells exceeding remaining spell levels.

entries.name: Insight (Very Rare)
entries.description: Wisdom score increases by 2 (max 20) while this incandescent blue sphere orbits your head.

entries.name: Intellect (Very Rare)
entries.description: Intelligence score increases by 2 (max 20) while this marbled scarlet and blue sphere orbits your head.

entries.name: Leadership (Very Rare)
entries.description: Charisma score increases by 2 (max 20) while this marbled pink and green sphere orbits your head.

entries.name: Mastery (Legendary)
entries.description: Proficiency bonus increases by 1 while this pale green prism orbits your head.

entries.name: Protection (Rare)
entries.description: Gain +1 bonus to AC while this dusty rose prism orbits your head.

entries.name: Regeneration (Legendary)
entries.description: Regain 15 hit points at the end of each hour this pearly white spindle orbits your head (requires at least 1 hit point).

entries.name: Reserve (Rare)
entries.description: Stores spells cast into it (up to 3 levels worth). When found, contains 1d4 1st-level spells chosen by the DM. Any creature can cast a spell of 1st–3rd level into the stone by touching it as the spell is cast. Stored spells can be cast while orbiting your head, using the original caster's slot level, spell save DC, and spell attack bonus.

entities.name: IRON BANDS OF BILARRO Wondrous item, rare

entries.name: Iron Bands of Bilarro
entries.description: This rusty iron sphere measures 3 inches in diameter and weighs 1 pound.

usage.content: Use an action to speak the command word and throw the sphere at a Huge or smaller creature you can see within 60 feet.
Make a ranged attack roll with an attack bonus equal to your Dexterity modifier plus your proficiency bonus.
On a hit, the target is restrained until you take a bonus action to speak the command word again to release it.
Doing so, or missing with the attack, causes the bands to contract and become a sphere once more.
A creature, including the one restrained, can use an action to make a DC {@dc 20} Strength check to break the iron bands.
On a success, the item is destroyed, and the restrained creature is freed. If the check fails, any further attempts made by that creature automatically fail until 24 hours have elapsed.
Once the bands are used, they can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Iron Bottle
entries.description: You can use an action to speak the flask's command word, targeting a creature you can see within 60 feet. If the target is native to a plane other than the one you're on, it must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or be trapped in the flask. If trapped before, it has advantage on the saving throw. A trapped creature remains until released, doesn't need to breathe/eat/drink, and doesn't age. Using an action to remove the stopper releases the creature, which is friendly for 1 hour and obeys commands. If no commands are given, it defends itself. Identify spell reveals a creature is inside, but type is only known by opening the flask. Newly discovered bottles may contain a DM-chosen or randomly determined creature.

entries.name: Well of Many Worlds
entries.description: This fine black cloth unfolds into a 6-foot diameter circle. Using an action to unfold and place it creates a two-way portal to another world/plane, with the DM deciding the destination. Closing the portal takes an action by folding the cloth. Once a portal is opened, the well cannot do so again for 1d8 hours.

entries.name: Javelin of Lightning
entries: A magic weapon that transforms into a lightning bolt when hurled with a command word. The bolt creates a 5-foot-wide line extending 120 feet from the caster. All creatures in the line (excluding the caster and target) must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On failure, they take {@damage 4d6} lightning damage; on success, they take half as much.

The lightning bolt reverts to a javelin upon reaching the target. Make a ranged weapon attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes damage from the javelin plus {@damage 4d6} lightning damage.
The javelin's transformation ability cannot be used again until the next dawn. During this cooldown period, the javelin can still be used as a magic weapon.

entries.name: Properties
entries: Weight: 1/2 pound
Size: 3 inches in diameter

entries.name: Usage
entries: As an action, one dose can be swallowed or applied to the skin

entries.name: Effects
entries: Regains {@healing 2d8+2} hit points
Ceases to be poisoned
Cured of any disease

entries.name: Lantern of Revealing
entries.description: While lit, this hooded lantern burns for 6 hours on 1 pint of oil, shedding bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Invisible creatures and objects are visible as long as they are in the lantern's bright light. You can use an action to lower the hood, reducing the light to dim light in a 5-foot radius.

entries.name: LUCK BLADE
entries.description: A legendary weapon (any sword) requiring attunement. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. While the sword is on your person, you also gain a +1 bonus to saving throws.

properties.name: Luck
properties.description: If the sword is on your person, you can call on its luck (no action required) to reroll one attack roll, ability check, or saving throw you dislike. You must use the second roll. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

properties.name: Wish
properties.description: The sword has 1d4-1 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cast the wish spell from it. This property can't be used again until the next dawn. The sword loses this property if it has no charges.

entries.name: Properties
entries: When you hit a fiend or an undead with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} radiant damage.

If the target has 25 hit points or fewer after taking this damage, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be destroyed. On a successful save, the creature becomes {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.
While you hold this weapon, it sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

entries.name: Mace of Smiting
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The bonus increases to +3 when you use the mace to attack a construct. When you roll a 20 on an attack roll made with this weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 7} bludgeoning damage, or an extra {@damage 14} bludgeoning damage if it's a construct. If a construct has 25 hit points or fewer after taking this damage, it is destroyed.

entries.name: MACE OF TERROR
entries: Weapon (mace), rare (requires attunement)

This magic weapon has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to release a wave of terror. Each creature of your choice in a 30-foot radius extending from you must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute.

While frightened in this way, a creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can, and it can't willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you. It also can't take reactions. For its action, it can use only the Dash action or try to escape from an effect that prevents it from moving. If it has nowhere it can move, the creature can use the Dodge action.
At the end of each of its turns, a creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.
The mace regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Mantle of Spell Resistance
entries: You have {@advantage} on saving throws against spells while you wear this cloak.

entities.name: MANUAL OF BODILY HEALTH Wondrous item, very rare

entries.name: Manual of Bodily Health
entries: This wondrous item contains health and diet tips imbued with magic. Spending 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying its contents and practicing its guidelines increases your Constitution score by 2 and your maximum Constitution score by 2. The manual loses its magic permanently after this effect, but regains its magic after a century.

entities.name: MANUAL OF GAINFUL EXERCISE Wondrous item, very rare

effects.name: Usage
entries: If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Strength score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score.

effects.name: Duration
entries: The manual then loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

entries.name: Manual of Golems
entries.description: This tome contains information and incantations necessary to make a particular type of golem. The DM chooses the type or determines it randomly. To decipher and use the manual, you must be a spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots. A creature that cannot use a manual of golems and attempts to read it takes {@damage 6d6 psychic} damage.
entries.usage: To create a golem, you must spend the time shown on the table (not included in source text), working without interruption with the manual at hand and resting no more than 8 hours per day. You must also pay the specified cost to purchase supplies.
entries.effect: Once you finish creating the golem, the book is consumed in eldritch flames. The golem becomes animate when the ashes of the manual are sprinkled on it. It is under your control, and it understands and obeys your spoken commands. See {@item Monster Manual|DMG} for its game statistics.
entries.requirement: Spellcaster with at least two 5th-level spell slots.
entries.special: The DM chooses the golem type or determines it randomly. Creation requires adherence to a table (not included in source text) for time and cost.

entities.name: M ANUAL OF QUICKNESS OF ACTION

entries.name: Description
entries: This book contains coordination and balance exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Dexterity score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The manual then loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

entries.name: Properties
entries: While wearing this armor, you have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
Whenever you start your turn underwater with 0 hit points, the armor causes you to rise 60 feet toward the surface.

entries.name: Medallion of Thoughts
entries.description: The medallion has 3 charges. While wearing it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast {@spell detect-thoughts} (save DC 13) from it. The medallion regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.
entries.action: Cast {@spell detect-thoughts} (save DC 13) with 1 expended charge

entries.name: Description
entries: Wondrous item, very rare

When this 4-foot-tall mirror is viewed indirectly, its surface shows faint images of creatures. The mirror weighs 50 pounds, and it has AC 11, 10 hit points, and vulnerability to bludgeoning damage. It shatters and is destroyed when reduced to 0 hit points.

entries.name: Activation
entries: If the mirror is hanging on a vertical surface and you are within 5 feet of it, you can use an action to speak its command word and activate it. It remains activated until you use an action to speak the command word again.

entries.name: Effects
entries: Any creature other than you that sees its reflection in the activated mirror while within 30 feet of it must succeed on a DC 15 Charisma saving throw or be trapped, along with anything it is wearing or carrying, in one of the mirror's twelve extradimensional cells. This saving throw is made with advantage if the creature knows the mirror's nature, and constructs succeed on the saving throw automatically.

An extradimensional cell is an infinite expanse filled with thick fog that reduces visibility to 10 feet. Creatures trapped in the mirror's cells don't age, and they don't need to eat, drink, or sleep. A creature trapped within a cell can escape using magic that permits planar travel. Otherwise, the creature is confined to the cell until freed.

entries.name: Behavior
entries: If the mirror traps a creature but its twelve extradimensional cells are already occupied, the mirror frees one trapped creature at random to accommodate the new prisoner. A freed creature appears in an unoccupied space within sight of the mirror but facing away from it. If the mirror is shattered, all creatures it contains are freed and appear in unoccupied spaces near it.

entries.name: Interactions
entries: While within 5 feet of the mirror, you can use an action to speak the name of one creature trapped in it or call out a particular cell by number. The creature named or contained in the named cell appears as an image on the mirror's surface. You and the creature can then communicate normally.

In a similar way, you can use an action to speak a second command word and free one creature trapped in the mirror. The freed creature appears, along with its possessions, in the unoccupied space nearest to the mirror and facing away from it.

entities.name: Armor (medium or heavy, but not hide),  uncommon

entries.text: Mithral is a light, flexible metal. A mithral chain shirt or breastplate can be worn under normal clothes.

entries.name: No Dexterity Disadvantage
entries.text: The mithral version of the armor doesn't impose disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

entries.name: No Strength Requirement
entries.text: The mithral version of the armor doesn't have a Strength requirement.

entries.name: Necklace of Adaptation
effects: While wearing this necklace, you can breathe normally in any environment.
You have advantage on saving throws made against harmful gases and vapors (such as cloudkill and stinking cloud effects, inhaled poisons, and the breath weapons of some dragons).

entities.name: NECKLACE OF FIREBALLS Wondrous item, rare

entries.name: Description
entries: This necklace has 1d6 + 3 beads hanging from it. You can use an action to detach a bead and throw it up to 6 feet away. When it reaches the end of its trajectory, the bead detonates as a {@damage 2d8 fire damage} fireball spell (save DC {@dc 15}).
You can hurl multiple beads, or even the whole necklace, as one action. When you do so, increase the level of the fireball by 1 for each bead beyond the first.

entries.name: NECKLACE OF PRAYER BEADS
entries.description: This necklace has 1d4 + 2 magic beads made from aquamarine, black pearl, or topaz. It also has many nonmagical beads made from stones such as amber, bloodstone, citrine, coral, jade, pearl, or quartz. If a magic bead is removed from the necklace, that bead loses its magic.
entries.use: Each magic bead contains a spell that you can cast from it as a bonus action (using your spell save DC if a save is necessary). Once a magic bead's spell is cast, that bead can't be used again until the next dawn.
entries.effect: Six types of magic beads exist. The DM decides the type of each bead on the necklace or determines it randomly. A necklace can have more than one bead of the same type.

entries.name: Nine Lives Stealer
entries.description: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The sword has 1d8 + 1 charges. If you score a critical hit against a creature that has fewer than 100 hit points, it must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be slain instantly as the sword tears its life force from its body (a construct or an undead is immune). The sword loses 1 charge if the creature is slain. When the sword has no charges remaining, it loses this property.

entities.name: NOLZUR'S MARVELOUS PIGMENTS Wondrous item, very rare

entries.name: Nolzur's Marvelous Pigments
entries.description: Typically found in 1d4 pots inside a fine wooden box with a brush (weighing 1 pound in total), these pigments allow you to create three-dimensional objects by painting them in two dimensions. The paint flows from the brush to form the desired object as you concentrate on its image. Each pot of paint is sufficient to cover 1,000 square feet of a surface, which lets you create inanimate objects or terrain features—such as a door, a pit, flowers, trees, cells, rooms, or weapons—that are up to 10,000 cubic feet. It takes 10 minutes to cover 100 square feet. When you complete the painting, the object or terrain feature depicted becomes a real, nonmagical object. Thus, painting a door on a wall creates an actual door that can be opened to whatever is beyond. Painting a pit on a floor creates a real pit, and its depth counts against the total area of objects you create. Nothing created by the pigments can have a value greater than 25 gp. If you paint an object of greater value (such as a diamond or a pile of gold), the object looks authentic, but close inspection reveals it is made from paste, bone, or some other worthless material. If you paint a form of energy such as fire or lightning, the energy appears but dissipates as soon as you complete the painting, doing no harm to anything.

entries.name: Description
entries: Weapon (longbow), very rare (requires attunement)
When you nock an arrow on this bow, it whispers in Elvish, "Swift defeat to my enemies."

When you use this weapon to make a ranged attack, you can, as a command phrase, say, "Swift death to you who have wronged me." The target of your attack becomes your sworn enemy until it dies or until dawn seven days later. You can have only one such sworn enemy at a time. When your sworn, enemy dies, you can choose a new one after the next dawn.

entries.name: Effects
entries: While your sworn enemy lives, you have disadvantage on attack rolls with all other weapons.

When you make a ranged attack roll with this weapon against your sworn enemy, you have advantage on the roll. In addition, your target gains no benefit from cover, other than total cover, and you suffer no disadvantage due to long range.
If the attack hits, your sworn enemy takes an extra {@damage 3d6} piercing damage.

entries.name: Oil of Etherealness Potion
entries.description: Beads of this cloudy gray oil form on the outside of its container and quickly evaporate. The oil can cover a Medium or smaller creature, along with the equipment it's wearing and carrying (one additional vial is required for each size category above Medium).

effects.name: Effect
entries: The affected creature gains the effect of the {@spell etherealness} spell.

entries.name: Description
entries: This clear, gelatinous oil sparkles with tiny, ultrathin silver shards. The oil can coat one slashing or piercing weapon or up to 5 pieces of slashing or piercing ammunition.

entries.name: Usage
entries: Applying the oil takes 1 minute.

entries.name: Duration
entries: 1 hour

entries.name: Effect
entries: The coated item is magical and has a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

entries.name: Potion
entries.description: This sticky black unguent is thick and heavy in the container, but it flows quickly when poured. The oil can cover a Medium or smaller creature, along with the equipment it's wearing and carrying (one additional vial is required for each size category above Medium). Applying the oil takes 10 minutes. The affected creature then gains the effect of a freedom of movement spell for 8 hours. Alternatively, the oil can be poured on the ground as an action, where it covers a 10-foot square, duplicating the effect of the grease spell in that area for 8 hours. This concoction looks, smells, and tastes like a potion of healing or other beneficial potion. However, it is actually poison masked by illusion magic. An identify spell reveals its true nature.
effects: When applied to a creature, grants resistance to one type of damage (chosen randomly by the DM) for 1 hour.

When consumed, causes 3d6 poison damage. A successful DC 13 Constitution saving throw negates the initial damage. On subsequent turns, the poisoned creature takes 3d6 poison damage at the start of their turn, with a chance to reduce damage by 1d6 per successful save (ending when damage decreases to 0).
Allows breathing underwater for 1 hour. Its cloudy green fluid smells of the sea and has a jellyfish-like bubble floating in it.

entries.name: PEARL OF POWER
entries.description: You can use an action to speak this pearl's command word and regain one expended spell slot of up to 3rd level. Once you have used the pearl, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entities.name: PERIAPT OF HEALTH Wondrous item, uncommon

entries.name: Periapt of Health
entries: You are immune to contracting any disease while you wear this pendant. If you are already infected with a disease, the effects of the disease are suppressed while you wear the pendant.

entities.name: PERIAPT OF PROOF AGAINST POISON Wondrous item, rare

entries.name: Periapt of Proof Against Poison
entries.description: This delicate silver chain has a brilliant-cut black gem pendant. While you wear it, poisons have no effect on you. You are immune to the poisoned condition and have immunity to poison damage.

entries.name: Periapt of Wound Closure
entries.description: While you wear this pendant, you stabilize whenever you are dying at the start of your turn. In addition, whenever you roll a Hit Die to regain hit points, double the number of hit points it restores.

entries.name: PHILTER OF LOVE
entries.description: The next time you see a creature within 10 minutes after drinking this philter, you become charmed by that creature for 1 hour. If the creature is of a species and gender you are normally attracted to, you regard it as your true love while you are charmed. This potion's rose-hued, effervescent liquid contains one easy-to-mis bubble shaped like a heart.

entries.name: Pipes of Haunting
entries: Type: Wondrous item
Rarity: Uncommon
Proficiency Required: Wind instruments
Charges: 3 (regain 1d3 charges daily at dawn)
Usage: Action (expend 1 charge to play)
Effect: Create an eerie, spellbinding tune. Each creature within 30 feet that hears the tune must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be frightened of you for 1 minute.
Optional Effect: You may allow all non-hostile creatures in the area to automatically succeed on the saving throw.
Condition: A creature that fails the saving throw can repeat it at the end of its turn to end the effect on itself.
Immunity: A creature that succeeds on its saving throw is immune to this effect for 24 hours.

entries.name: Pipes of the Sewers
description: You must be proficient with wind instruments to use these pipes. While you are attuned to the pipes, ordinary rats and giant rats are indifferent toward you and will not attack you unless you threaten or harm them.

description.name: Charge Mechanics
entries: The pipes have 3 charges. If you play the pipes as an action, you can use a bonus action to expend 1 to 3 charges, calling forth one swarm of rats (see the Monster Manual for statistics) with each expended charge, provided that enough rats are within half a mile of you to be called in this fashion (as determined by the DM).

If there aren't enough rats to form a swarm, the charge is wasted. Called swarms move toward the music by the shortest available route but aren't under your control otherwise. The pipes regain 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

description.name: Swarm Interaction
entries: Whenever a swarm of rats that isn't under another creature's control comes within 30 feet of you while you are playing the pipes, you can make a Charisma check contested by the swarm's Wisdom check.

If you lose the contest, the swarm behaves as it normally would and can't be swayed by the pipes' music for the next 24 hours. If you win the contest, the swarm is swayed by the pipes' music and becomes friendly to you and your companions for as long as you continue to play the pipes each round as an action.

A friendly swarm obeys your commands. If you issue no commands to a friendly swarm, it defends itself but otherwise takes no actions. If a friendly swarm starts its turn and can't hear the pipes' music, your control over that swarm ends, and the swarm behaves as it normally would and can't be swayed by the pipes' music for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: PLATE ARMOR OF ETHEREALNESS
entries.description: While you're wearing this armor, you can speak its command word as an action to gain the effect of the etherealness spell, which lasts for 10 minutes or until you remove the armor or use an action to speak the command word again. This property of the armor can't be used again until the next dawn.

properties.name: Etherealness
properties.description: You can speak its command word as an action to gain the effect of the etherealness spell, which lasts for 10 minutes or until you remove the armor or use an action to speak the command word again. This property of the armor can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Portable Hole
entries.description: A fine black cloth folded to the size of a handkerchief, unfolding into a circular sheet 6 feet in diameter. When unfolded, it creates an extradimensional hole 10 feet deep on a different plane, incapable of forming open passages. Creatures inside can climb out.

mechanics.name: Unfold Portable Hole
mechanics.description: Use an action to place the folded cloth on a solid surface, creating an extradimensional hole 10 feet deep. The cylindrical space exists on a separate plane.

mechanics.name: Close Portable Hole
mechanics.description: Use an action to fold the cloth, closing the hole. Contents remain in the extradimensional space. The closed hole weighs next to nothing.

mechanics.name: Escape Closed Portable Hole
mechanics.description: A creature inside a closed hole can use an action to make a {@dc 10} Strength check. On success, it exits within 5 feet of the hole or carrier.

mechanics.name: Suffocation in Closed Hole
mechanics.description: A breathing creature in a closed hole can survive up to 10 minutes before suffocating.

mechanics.name: Interaction with Extradimensional Containers
mechanics.description: Placing a portable hole inside a bag of holding, Heward's handy haversack, or similar item instantly destroys both and opens a one-way gate to the Astral Plane. Creatures within 10 feet of the gate are sucked to a random location on the Astral Plane.

entries.name: POTION OF ANIMAL FRIENDSHIP
entries.description: When you drink this potion, you can cast the animal friendship spell {@item Animal Friendship|PHB} (save DC {@dc 13}) for 1 hour at will. Agitating this muddy liquid brings little bits into view: a fish scale, a hummingbird tongue, a cat claw, or a squirrel hair.

entries.name: Potion of Clairvoyance
entries.description: An eyeball bobs in this yellowish liquid but vanishes when the potion is opened.
entries.effect: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell clairvoyance} spell.

entries.name: Potion, common
entries.description: When you drink this potion, you gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed for 1 hour. During this time, you have advantage on Strength (Athletics) checks you make to climb. The potion is separated into brown, silver, and gray layers resembling bands of stone. Shaking the bottle fails to mix the colors.

entries.name: Potion of Diminution
entries.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the "reduce" effect of the enlarge/reduce spell for 1d4 hours (no concentration required). The red in the potion's liquid continuously contracts to a tiny bead and then expands to color the clear liquid around it. Shaking the bottle fails to interrupt this process.

entries.name: Potion of Fire Breath
entries.description: This potion's orange liquid flickers, and smoke fills the top of the container and wafts out whenever it is opened.
effects: Use a bonus action to exhale fire at a target within 30 feet. The target must make a {@dc 13} Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, they take {@damage 4d6 fire} damage; on a successful save, half damage.
The effect lasts until you exhale fire three times or 1 hour has passed.

entries: When you drink this potion, you gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed for 1 hour and can hover. If you're in the air when the potion wears off, you fall unless you have some other means of staying aloft.
This potion's clear liquid floats at the top of its container and has cloudy white impurities drifting in it.

entities.name: POTION OF GASEOUS FORM Potion, rare

description.name: Effect
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@item Gaseous Form|PHB.spell.gaseous-form} spell for {@duration 1 hour} (no concentration required) or until you end the effect as a {@action bonus action}.

description.name: Container Description
entries: This potion's container seems to hold fog that moves and pours like water.

entries.name: Potion of Giant Strength
entries.description: This potion's transparent liquid contains a sliver of fingernail from a giant of the appropriate type. The potion of frost giant strength and the potion of stone giant strength have the same effect.

effects.name: Duration
entries: 1 hour

effects.name: Strength Change
entries: Your Strength score changes based on the type of giant (see the table below). The potion has no effect if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.

entries.name: Potion of Growth
entries.description: When you drink this potion, you gain the 'enlarge' effect of the enlarge/reduce spell for 1d4 hours (no concentration required). The red in the potion's liquid continuously expands from a tiny bead to color the clear liquid around it and then contracts. Shaking the bottle fails to interrupt this process.

entries.name: Potion of Healing
entries.description: The potion's red liquid glimmers when agitated.
entries.effect: Regain hit points. The number of hit points depends on the potion's rarity, as shown in the Potions of Healing table.

entries.name: Potion of Invisibility
entries.description: This potion's container looks empty but feels as though it holds liquid. When you drink it, you become invisible for 1 hour. Anything you wear or carry is invisible with you. The effect ends early if you attack or cast a spell.

entries.name: Potion of Longevity
entries.description: Suspended in this amber liquid are a scorpion's tail, an adder's fang, a dead spider, and a tiny heart that, against all reason, is still beating. These ingredients vanish when the potion is opened. The potion's crimson liquid regularly pulses in dulllight, calling to mind a heartbeat.

effects.name: Age Reduction
entries: When you drink this potion, your physical age is reduced by 1d6 + 6 years, to a minimum of 13 years.
Each time you subsequently drink a potion of longevity, there is 10 percent cumulative chance that you instead age by 1d6 + 6 years.

effects.name: Haste Effect
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the haste spell for 1 minute (no concentration required).

effects.name: Healing and Recovery
entries: When you drink this potion, it removes any exhaustion you are suffering and cures any disease or poison affecting you.
For the next 24 hours, you regain the maximum number of hit points for any Hit Die you spend.

entries.name: Quaal's Feather Token
entries: A rare wondrous item that manifests as a tiny feather. The DM selects its type or determines it randomly.

entries.name: Anchor
entries: Use an action to touch the token to a boat or ship. The vessel cannot be moved by any means for 24 hours. Touching the token again ends the effect. The token disappears when the effect ends.
{@duration 24h}

entries.name: Bird
entries: Use an action to toss the token 5 feet into the air. The token disappears, and an enormous, multicolored bird (statistics of a roc from the Monster Manual) appears. It obeys your commands, cannot attack, and can carry up to 500 pounds at 16 mph (max 144 miles/day with 1-hour rests every 3 hours of flight). The bird disappears after flying its maximum distance or if it drops to 0 hit points. You can dismiss it as an action.
{@action dismiss}

entries.name: Fan
entries: While on a boat or ship, use an action to toss the token up to 10 feet. The token disappears, and a giant fan appears, filling sails to increase the ship's speed by 5 mph for 8 hours. You can dismiss the fan as an action.

entries.name: Swan Boat
entries: Use an action to touch the token to a body of water at least 60 feet in diameter. The token disappears, and a 50-foot-long, 20-foot-wide swan-shaped boat appears. It moves at 6 mph, can carry 32 Medium or smaller creatures (Large = 4 Medium, Huge = 9 Medium), and remains for 24 hours. You can command its movement or turn as an action. The boat disappears after 24 hours or if dismissed.

entries.name: Tree
entries: Must be outdoors. Use an action to touch the token to an unoccupied space. The token disappears, and a nonmagical oak tree (60 feet tall, 5-foot-diameter trunk) springs into existence. Its branches spread in a 20-foot radius.

entries.name: Whip
entries: Use an action to throw the token to a point within 10 feet. The token disappears, and a floating whip appears. On your next turn, use a bonus action to make a melee spell attack with +9 bonus against a creature within 10 feet of the whip, dealing 1d6+5 force damage. As a bonus action, direct the whip up to 20 feet to repeat the attack. The whip disappears after 1 hour, when dismissed, or if you are incapacitated/dead.

entities.name: QUIVER OF EHLONNA Wondrous item, uncommon

entries.name: Quiver of Ehlonna
entries.description: Each of the quiver's three compartments connects to an extradimensional space that allows the quiver to hold numerous items while never weighing more than 2 pounds. The shortest compartment can hold up to sixty arrows, bolts, or similar objects. The midsize compartment holds up to eighteen javelins or similar objects. The longest compartment holds up to six long objects, such as bows, quarterstaffs, or spears. You can draw any item the quiver contains as if doing so from a regular quiver or scabbard.

entities.name: RING OF ANIMAL INFLUENCE Ring, rare

entries.name: Charges
entries: The ring has 3 charges. It regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Ability
entries: While wearing the ring, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells:

items: Fear {@dc 13} (targets only beasts with Intelligence 3 or lower)

entries.name: Ring of Djinni Summoning
entries.description:
While wearing this ring, you can speak its command word as an action to summon a particular djinni from the Elemental Plane of Air. The djinn, appears in an unoccupied space you choose within 120 feet of you. It remains as long as you concentrate (as if concentrating on a spell), to a maximum of 1 hour, or until it drops to 0 hit points. It then returns to its home plane.

While summoned, the djinni is friendly to you and your companions. It obeys any commands you give it, no matter what language you use. If you fail to command it, the djinni defends itself against attackers but takes no other actions.

After the djinni departs, it can't be summoned again for 24 hours, and the ring becomes nonmagical if the djinni dies.

entries.name: Ring of Elemental Command
entries.description: This ring is linked to one of the four Elemental Planes. The DM chooses or randomly determines the linked plane. While wearing this ring, you have advantage on attack rolls against elementals from the linked plane, and they have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. In addition, you have access to properties based on the linked plane.

entries.name: Air Elemental Command
entries: You can expend 2 of the ring's charges to cast dominate monster on an air elemental.
When you fall, you descend 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.
You can speak and understand Auran.

entries.name: Additional Properties (Air Elemental)
entries: Resistance to lightning damage.
Flying speed equal to your walking speed and can hover.
Cast the following spells from the ring, expending the necessary number of charges: chain lightning (3 charges), gust of wind (2 charges), or wall of water (1 charge).

entries.name: Earth Elemental Command
entries: You can expend 2 of the ring's charges to cast dominate monster on an earth elemental.
Move in difficult terrain composed of rubble, rocks, or dirt as if it were normal terrain.
You can speak and understand Terran.

entries.name: Additional Properties (Earth Elemental)
entries: Resistance to acid damage.
Move through solid earth or rock as if the areas were difficult terrain. If you end your turn while within such terrain, you are shunted out to the nearest unoccupied space you last occupied.
Cast the following spells from the ring, expending the necessary number of charges: stone shape (2 charges), stoneskin (3 charges), or wall of stone (3 charges).

entries.name: Fire Elemental Command
entries: You can expend 2 of the ring's charges to cast dominate monster on a fire elemental.
Resistance to fire damage.
You can speak and understand Ignan.

entries.name: Additional Properties (Fire Elemental)
entries: Immune to fire damage.
Cast the following spells from the ring, expending the necessary number of charges: burning hands (1 charge), fireball (2 charges), and wall of fire (3 charges).

entries.name: Water Elemental Command
entries: You can expend 2 of the ring's charges to cast dominate monster on a water elemental.
Stand on and walk across liquid surfaces as if they were solid ground.
You can speak and understand Aquan.

entries.name: Additional Properties (Water Elemental)
entries: Can breathe underwater and have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed.
Cast the following spells from the ring, expending the necessary number of charges: create or destroy water (1 charge), control water (3 charges), ice storm (2 charges), or wall of ice (3 charges).

entries.name: Ring of Evasion
entries: A ring that has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. When you fail a Dexterity saving throw while wearing it, you can use your reaction to expend 1 of its charges to succeed on that saving throw instead.

entries.name: Ring of Feather Falling
entries.description: When you fall while wearing this ring, you descend 60 feet per round and take no damage from falling.

entries.name: Ring
entries.description: While you wear this ring, difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement. In addition, magic can neither reduce your speed nor cause you to be paralyzed or restrained.

entry.description: While wearing this ring, you can turn invisible as an action. Anything you are wearing or carrying is invisible with you. You remain invisible until the ring is removed, until you attack or cast a spell, or until you use a bonus action to become visible again.
entry.duration: until the ring is removed, until you attack or cast a spell, or until you use a bonus and action to become visible again
effect: Carried and worn items become invisible with the wearer

entries.name: Ring of Jumping
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you can cast the jump spell from it as a bonus action at will, but can target only yourself when you do so.

entries.name: Ring of Mind Shielding
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you are immune to magic that allows other creatures to read your thoughts, determine whether you are lying, know your alignment, or know your creature type. Creatures can telepathically communicate with you only if you allow it.

entries.name: Invisibility
entries: You can use an action to cause the ring to become invisible until you use another action to make it visible, until you remove the ring, or until you die.

entries.name: Soul Entry
entries: If you die while wearing the ring, your soul enters it, unless it already houses a soul. You can remain in the ring or depart for the afterlife. As long as your soul is in the ring, you can telepathically communicate with any creature wearing it. A wearer can't prevent this telepathic communication.

entries.name: Ring of Protection
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws while wearing this ring.

entries.name: RING OF REGENERATION
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you regain 1d6 hit points every 10 minutes, provided that you have at least 1 hit point. If you lose a body part, the ring causes the missing part to regrow and return to full functionality after 1d6 + 1 days if you have at least 1 hit point the whole time.

entries.name: Ring of Resistance
entries.description: You have resistance to one damage type while wearing this ring. The gem in the ring indicates the type, which the DM chooses or determines randomly.

entries.name: Ring of Shooting Stars
entries: A very rare ring that requires attunement to use outdoors at night.

entries.name: Faerie Fire
entries: Expends 1 charge as an action to cast Faerie Fire from the ring.

entries.name: Ball Lightning
entries: Expends 2 charges as an action to create 1-4 spheres of lightning (each sphere is 3-foot-diameter).
Spheres last up to 1 minute (concentration), shedding dim light in a 30-foot radius.
As a bonus action, move each sphere up to 30 feet (max 120 feet from you).
On a creature entering 5 feet of a sphere, the sphere discharges lightning: {@dc 15} Dexterity save. On fail, target takes {@damage 2d6} lightning damage (reduced by half on success).

entries.name: Shooting Stars
entries: Expends 1-3 charges as an action. For each charge, launch a glowing mote of light within 60 feet.
Creatures in a 15-foot cube from the mote must make {@dc 15} Dexterity save. On fail, take {@damage 5d4} fire damage (half on success).

entries.name: Charge Management
entries: The ring has 6 charges for its properties.
Regains 1d6 expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Ring of Spell Storing
entries: A rare ring that stores spells cast into it, holding them until the attuned wearer uses them. The ring can store up to 5 levels worth of spells at a time. When found, it contains ld6 I levels of stored spells chosen by the DM.

items: Any creature can cast a spell of 1st through 5th level into the ring by touching the ring as the spell is cast. The spell has no effect, other than to be stored in the ring.
If the ring cannot hold the spell, the spell is expendec without effect. The level of the slot used to cast the spell determines how much space it uses.

entries.name: Usage
entries: While wearing this ring, you can cast any spell stored in it. The spell uses the slot level, spell save DC, spell attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the original caster, but is otherwise treated as if you cast the spell.
The spell cast from the ring is no longer stored in it, freeing up space.

entries.name: Ring of Spell Turning
description: While wearing this ring, you have advantage on saving throws against any spell that targets only you (not in an area of effect).

In addition, if you roll a 20 for the save and the spell is 7th level or lower, the spell has no effect on you and instead targets the caster, using the slot level, spell save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability of the caster.

effects.name: Saving Throw Advantage
entries: You gain advantage on saving throws against spells targeting only you (not in an area of effect).

effects.name: Spell Redirect
entries: On a successful save (rolling a 20) against a 7th-level or lower spell, the spell instead targets the caster. The spell uses the caster's slot level, {@dc} spell save DC, attack bonus, and spellcasting ability.

entries.name: Ring of Swimming
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you have a swimming speed of {@speed 40 feet}.

entries.name: Ring of Telekinesis
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you can cast the {@spell Telekinesis|PHB} spell at will, but you can target only objects that aren't being worn or carried.
limitations: Target objects cannot be worn or carried

entries.name: Attack with Spectral Ram
entries.description: Use an action to expend 1-3 charges. The ring makes an attack roll with a +7 bonus. On a hit, each charge spent deals {@damage 2d10 force} and pushes the target 5 feet away {@condition pushed 5 feet}.

entries.name: Break Object
entries.description: Use an action to expend 1-3 charges. The ring makes a Strength check with a +5 bonus per charge spent. The target must be an object within 60 feet that isn't being worn or carried.

entries.name: Ring of Three Wishes
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to expend 1 of its 3 charges to cast the wish spell from it. The ring becomes nonmagical when you use the last charge.

entries.name: Ring of Warmth
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you have resistance to cold damage. You and everything you wear and carry are unharmed by temperatures as low as -50 degrees Fahrenheit.

entries.name: Ring of Water Walking
entries: While wearing this ring, you can stand on and move across any liquid surface as if it were solid ground.

entries.name: Ring of X-Ray Vision
entries.description: While wearing this ring, you can use an action to speak its command word. When you do so, you can see into and through solid matter for 1 minute. This vision has a radius of 30 feet. To you, solid objects within that radius appear transparent and don't prevent light from passing through them. The vision can penetrate 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, or up to 3 feet of wood or dirt. Thicker substances block the vision, as does a thin sheet of lead.

properties.name: Command Word
properties.description: Use an action to speak the command word to activate the vision.

properties.name: Vision
properties.description: See through solid matter for 1 minute with a 30-foot radius. Solid objects appear transparent, and the vision can penetrate 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of common metal, or up to 3 feet of wood or dirt. Thicker substances or a thin sheet of lead block the vision.

properties.name: Exhaustion Consequence
properties.description: If you use the ring again before taking a long rest, you must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or gain one level of exhaustion.

entries.name: ROBE OF EYES
entries.description: While you wear the robe, you gain the following benefits: You can see in all directions and have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight. You have darkvision out to 120 feet. You can see invisible creatures and objects, as well as see into the Ethereal Plane out to 120 feet. The eyes on the robe cannot be closed or averted; you are never considered to be closing or averting them. A light spell cast on the robe or a daylight spell cast within 5 feet of the robe causes you to be blinded for 1 minute. At the end of each of your turns, you can make a Constitution saving throw (DC 11 for light or DC 15 for daylight) to end the blindness on a success.

entities.name: ROBE OF SCINTILLATING COLORS Wondrous item, very rare (requires attunement)

entries.name: Robe of Scintillating Colors
entries.description: This robe has 3 charges and regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn. While wearing it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge, causing the garment to display shifting hues until the end of your next turn. The robe sheds bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. Creatures that can see you have disadvantage on attack rolls against you. Any creature in the bright light that sees you when the power is activated must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw or be stunned until the effect ends.

entries.name: ROBE OF STARS
entries.description: This black or dark blue robe is embroidered with small white or silver stars.

properties.value: Once per day, you can use an action to pull off one of the six large stars and cast magic missile as a 5th-level spell. The removed stars reappear daily at dusk.

As an action, you can enter the Astral Plane along with everything you are wearing and carrying. You remain there until you use an action to return to the plane you were on, reappearing in the last space you occupied or the nearest unoccupied space.

entries.name: Robe of the Archmagi
entries.attunement: Requires attunement by a sorcerer, warlock, or wizard
entries.description: This elegant garment is made from exquisite cloth of white, gray, or black and adorned with silvery runes. The robe's color corresponds to the alignment for which the item was created: white for good, gray for neutral, and black for evil. You cannot attune to a robe of the Archmagi that doesn't correspond to your alignment.
benefits: If you aren't wearing armor, your base Armor Class is 15 + your Dexterity modifier.
You have advantage on saving throws against spell and other magical effects.
Your spell save DC and spell attack bonus each increase by 2.

entries.name: Robe of Useful Items
entries.description: While wearing the robe, you can use an action to detach one of the patches, causing it to become the object or creature it represents. Once the last patch is removed, the robe becomes an ordinary garment. The robe has two of each of the following patches: Dagger, Bullseye lantern (filled and lit), Steel mirror, 10-foot pole, Hempen rope (50 feet, coiled), and Sack. In addition, the robe has 4d4 other patches. The DM chooses the patches or determines them randomly.

entries.name: Patches
entries: Dagger
Bullseye lantern (filled and lit)
Steel mirror
10-foot pole
Hempen rope (50 feet, coiled)
Sack

entries.name: Additional Patches
items: 4d4 other patches (DM chooses or determines randomly)

entries.name: Absorption
entries: While holding this rod, you can use your reaction to absorb a spell targeting only you. The spell's effect is canceled, and its energy (not the spell itself) is stored in the rod. The energy retains the level of the spell when it was cast.
The rod can store up to 50 levels of spell energy over its existence. Once full, it cannot absorb more. If a spell cannot be stored, the rod has no effect on it.

entries.name: Attunement
entries: When attuned, you know the rod's total stored energy and its current spell energy level.

As a spellcaster, you can convert stored energy into spell slots. Created slots are equal to or lower than your own spell slots, up to 5th level. For example, 3 stored levels can be used as a 3rd-level slot.

entries.name: Properties
entries: A newly found rod has 10 levels of energy stored. If the rod can no longer absorb energy and has none remaining, it becomes nonmagical.

entities.name: Ron OF ALERTNESS Rod, very rare (requires attunement)

entries.name: Alertness
entries.content: While holding the rod, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and on rolls for initiative.

entries.name: Protective Aura
entries.content: As an action, you can plant the haft end of the rod in the ground, whereupon the rod's head sheds bright light in a 60-foot radius and dim light for an additional 60 feet. While in that bright light, you and any creature that is friendly to you gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws and can sense the location of any invisible hostile creature that is also in the bright light. The rod's head stops glowing and the effect ends after 10 minutes, or when a creature uses an action to pull the rod from the ground. This property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entities.name: Ron oF LoRDLY MIGHT Rod, legendary (requires attunement)

entries.name: Description
entries: This rod has a flanged head and functions as a magic mace that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. It features six buttons along the haft, each activating distinct properties.
The rod has three additional properties: Drain Life, Paralyze, and Terrify.

entries.name: Buttons
entries: Six Buttons: Press one of the rod's six buttons as a bonus action. A button's effect lasts until you press a different button or press the same button again, reverting the rod to its normal form.
Button 1: Transforms the rod into a flame tongue, sprouting a fiery blade from the end opposite the flanged head.
Button 2: The flanged head folds down, revealing two crescent-shaped blades, transforming it into a magic battleaxe with a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.
Button 3: The flanged head folds down, a spear point springs from the tip, and the handle lengthens into a 6-foot haft, becoming a magic spear with a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls.

Button 4: Transforms into a climbing pole up to 50 feet long. In hard surfaces like granite, it features a spike at the bottom and three hooks at the top, with horizontal bars forming a ladder. The pole can bear up to 4,000 pounds. Reverts to normal form if there is no solid anchoring or weight.
Button 5: Transforms into a handheld battering ram, granting a +10 bonus to Strength checks to break through doors, barricades, and barriers.

Button 6: Returns the rod to its normal form and indicates magnetic north. If used in a location without magnetic north, nothing happens. Also provides knowledge of approximate depth beneath the ground or height above it.

items: Drain Life: On a successful melee attack, force the target to make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On failure, the target takes 4d6 necrotic damage and you regain half that damage in hit points. Cannot be used again until dawn.

Paralyze: On a successful melee attack, force the target to make a DC 17 Strength saving throw. On failure, the target is paralyzed for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each turn to end the effect. Cannot be used again until dawn.

Terrify: While holding the rod, use an action to force each creature within 30 feet to make a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw. On failure, the target is frightened of you for 1 minute. A frightened target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each turn to end the effect. Cannot be used again until dawn.

entities.name: R on OF THE PACT KEEPER

entries.name: Rod of the Pact Keeper
entries: A rod of the pact keeper is available in uncommon (+1), rare (+2), or very rare (+3) versions. It requires attunement by a warlock.

While holding this rod, you gain a bonus to spell attack rolls and to the saving throw DCs of your warlock spells. The bonus is determined by the rod's rarity: +1 for uncommon, +2 for rare, and +3 for very rare.
You can regain one warlock spell slot as an action while holding the rod. You cannot use this property again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Rod of Resurrection
entries: A legendary magic item that requires attunement by a cleric, druid, or paladin.

items: On reaching 0 charges, roll a d20. On a 1, the rod disappears in a burst of radiance

entries.name: Usage
entries: While holding the rod, use an action to cast one of the following spells from it:

entries.name: Rod of Rulership
entries.description: This rod requires attunement. You can use an action to present the rod and command obedience from each creature of your choice that you can see within 120 feet of you. Each target must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for 8 hours. While charmed in this way, the creature regards you as its trusted leader. If harmed by you or your companions, or commanded to do something contrary to its nature, a target ceases to be charmed. The rod can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Rod of Security
entries.description: While holding this rod, you can use an action to activate it. The rod then instantly transports you and up to 199 other willing creatures you can see to a paradise that exists in an extraplanar space. You choose the form that the paradise takes. It could be a tranquil garden, lovely glade, cheery tavern, immense palace, tropical island, fantastic carnival, or whatever else you can imagine. Regardless of its nature, the paradise contains enough water and food to sustain its visitors. Everything else that can be interacted with inside the extraplanar space can exist only there. For example, a flower picked from a garden in the paradise disappears if it is taken outside the extraplanar space.

entries.name: Hit Points Regain
entries: For each hour spent in the paradise, a visitor regains hit points as if it had spent 1 Hit Die.

entries.name: Aging
entries: Creatures don't age while in the paradise, although time passes normally.

entries.name: Duration
entries: Visitors can remain in the paradise for up to 200 days divided by the number of creatures present (round down).

entries.name: Ending the Paradise
entries: When the time runs out or you use an action to end it, all visitors reappear in the location they occupied when you activated the rod, or an unoccupied space nearest that location.

entries.name: Cooldown
entries: The rod can't be used again until ten days have passed.

entries.name: Properties
entries: A 60-foot length of silk rope weighing 3 pounds, capable of holding up to 3,000 pounds.
When commanded, the rope animates and moves as described below.

As a bonus action, you can command the other end to move toward a destination you choose. That end moves 10 feet on your turn when first commanded and 10 feet on each of your subsequent turns until reaching its destination, up to its maximum length away, or until told to stop.
You can instruct the rope to fasten/unfasten itself, knot/unknot itself, or coil itself for carrying.
If told to knot, large knots appear at 1-foot intervals, shortening the rope to 50 feet and granting advantage on climb checks.
The rope has AC 20 and 20 hit points. It regains 1 hit point every 5 minutes while alive. If reduced to 0 hit points, it is destroyed.

entries.name: Rope of Entanglement
entries: Wondrous item, rare

This rope is 30 feet long and weighs 3 pounds. If you hold one end of the rope and use an action to speak its command word, the other end darts forward to entangle a creature you can see within 20 feet of you. The target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition prone} and {@condition restrained}.

You can release the creature by using a bonus action to speak a second command word. A target restrained by the rope can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Strength or Dexterity check (target's choice). On a success, the creature is no longer restrained by the rope.
The rope has AC 20 and 20 hit points. It regains 1 hit point every 5 minutes as long as it has at least 1 hit point. If the rope drops to 0 hit points, it is destroyed.

entries.name: Saddle of the Cavalier
entries: While in this saddle on a mount, you can't be dismounted against your will if you're conscious
Attack rolls against the mount have disadvantage

entries.name: Scarab of Protection
entries.description: If you hold this beetle-shaped medallion in your hand for 1 round, an inscription appears on its surface revealing its magical nature. While it is on your person, you have advantage on saving throws against spells. The scarab has 12 charges. If you fail a saving throw against a necromancy spell or a harmful effect originating from an undead creature, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and turn the failed save into a successful one. The scarab crumbles into powder and is destroyed when its last charge is expended.

entries.name: SCIMITAR OF SPEED
effects: +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon
Make one attack with it as a bonus action on each of your turns

entries.name: Scroll of Protection
entries.description: Each scroll of protection works against a specific type of creature chosen by the DM or determined randomly by rolling on the following table. Using an action to read the scroll encloses you in an invisible barrier that extends from you to form a 5-foot-radius, 10-foot-high cylinder. For 5 minutes, this barrier prevents creatures of the specified type from entering or affecting anything within the cylinder. The cylinder moves with you and remains centered on you. However, if you move in such a way that a creature of the specified type would be inside the cylinder, the effect ends. A creature can attempt to overcome the barrier by using an action to make a DC 15 Charisma check. On a success, the creature ceases to be affected by the barrier.

effect.name: Barrier Creation
entries: An invisible barrier forms a 5-foot-radius, 10-foot-high cylinder centered on you.
The barrier lasts for 5 minutes and prevents creatures of the specified type from entering or affecting the cylinder.

effect.name: Movement and Duration
entries: The cylinder moves with you, but the effect ends if you move in a way that a creature of the specified type would be inside the cylinder.
The barrier persists for 5 minutes.

effect.name: Overcoming the Barrier
entries: A creature can use an action to make a DC 15 Charisma check to overcome the barrier. On a success, the creature is no longer affected by the barrier.

Sending stones come in pairs, with each smooth stone carved to match the other so the pairing is easily recognized. While you touch one stone, you can use an action to cast the sending spell from it. The target is the bearer of the other stone. If no creature bears the other stone, you know that fact as soon as you use the stone and don't cast the spell.
Once sending is cast through the stones, they can't be used again until the next dawn. If one of the stones in a pair is destroyed, the other one becomes nonmagical.

entries.name: SENTINEL SHIELD
entries.description: While holding this shield, you have advantage on initiative rolls and Wisdom (Perception) checks. The shield is emblazoned with a symbol of an eye.

entries.name: SHIELD
entries.description: While holding this shield, you have a bonus to AC determined by the shield's rarity. This bonus is in addition to the shield's normal bonus to AC.

entries.name: Shield of Missile Attraction
entries.description: While holding this shield, you have resistance to damage from ranged weapon attacks. Curse: Attuning to it curses you until you are targeted by the remove curse spell or similar magic. Removing the shield fails to end the curse on you. Whenever a ranged weapon attack is made against a target within 10 feet of you, the curse causes you to become the target instead.

entries.name: Slippers of Spider Climbing
entries.description: While you wear these light shoes, you can move up, down, and across vertical surfaces and upside down along ceilings, while leaving your hands free. You have a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. However, the slippers don't allow you to move this way on a slippery surface, such as one covered by ice or oil.

entries.name: Sovereign Glue
entries.description: This viscous, milky-white substance can form a permanent adhesive bond between any two objects. It must be stored in a jar or flask that has been coated inside with oil of slipperiness. When found, a container contains 1d6 + 1 ounces. One ounce of the glue can cover a 1-foot square surface. The glue takes 1 minute to set. Once set, the bond can only be broken by universal solvent, oil of etherealness, or a wish spell.

properties.name: Adhesive Bond
properties.description: Forms a permanent bond between objects after 1 minute of setting. Requires storage in oil-coated container.

properties.name: Breakable Bond
properties.description: Once set, the bond can be broken by universal solvent, oil of etherealness, or a wish spell.

properties.name: Storage Requirement
properties.description: Must be stored in a jar or flask coated with oil of slipperiness.

entries.name: Spell Scroll
entries.description: A spell scroll contains a single spell written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can use an action to cast it without providing components. Higher-level spells require an ability check (DC = 10 + spell level) to cast successfully. Once cast, the scroll fades and crumbles to dust. The spell's level determines its saving throw DC, attack bonus, and the scroll's rarity (see Spell Scroll table). Wizard spells on scrolls can be copied with an Intelligence (Arcana) check (DC = 10 + spell level), destroying the scroll regardless of success.

rules.name: Casting a Spell Scroll
entries: If the spell is on your class's spell list, you can use an action to cast it without providing components.

If the spell is on your class's list but of a higher level than you can normally cast, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability (DC = 10 + spell level). On failure, the spell disappears with no effect.

rules.name: Scroll Destruction
entries: After casting a spell, the scroll fades and crumbles to dust.

rules.name: Spell Scroll Rarity
entries: The spell's level determines its saving throw DC, attack bonus, and the scroll's rarity (see {@item Spell Scroll Table|dmg.item.Spell_Scroll_Table}).

rules.name: Copying a Spell Scroll
entries: A wizard spell on a scroll can be copied like a spellbook entry. The copier must succeed on an Intelligence (Arcana) check (DC = 10 + spell level) to copy it. The scroll is destroyed regardless of the check's outcome.

entries.name: Spellguard Shield
entries.description: While holding this shield, you have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects, and spell attacks have disadvantage against you.

entities.name: SPHERE OF ANNIHILATION Wondrous item, legendary

entries.name: Sphere of Annihilation
entries.description: This 2-foot-diameter black sphere is a hole in the multiverse, hovering in space and stabilized by a magical field surrounding it. {@damage 4d10} force damage.

mechanics.name: Control Mechanics
entries: If you are within 60 feet of an uncontrolled sphere, you can use an action to make a DC 25 Intelligence (Arcana) check. On a success, the sphere levitates in one direction of your choice, up to a number of feet equal to 5 × your Intelligence modifier (minimum 5 feet). On a failure, the sphere moves 10 feet toward you.
A creature whose space the sphere enters must succeed on a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or be touched by it, taking {@damage 4d10} force damage.

mechanics.name: Contest for Control
entries: If you attempt to control a sphere that is under another creature's control, you make an Intelligence (Arcana) check contested by the other creature's Intelligence (Arcana) check. The winner gains control and can levitate the sphere as normal.

mechanics.name: Planar Interaction
entries: If the sphere contacts a planar portal (e.g., from *gate*) or extradimensional space (e.g., a portable hole), the DM determines random effects using a table (not included here).

entries.name: dlOO Result
entries: 01-50 The sphere is destroyed.
51 - 85 The sphere moves through the portal or into the extradimensional space.
86-00 A spatial rift sends each creature and object within 180 feet of the sphere, including the sphere, to a random plane of existence.

entries.name: Staff of Charming
entries: A rare magic staff that requires attunement by a bard, cleric, druid, sorcerer, warlock, or wizard. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its 10 charges to cast charm person, command, or comprehend languages using your spell save DC. The staff can also be used as a magic quarterstaff.

items: If you fail a saving throw against an enchantment spell targeting only you while holding the staff, you can turn your failed save into a success by expending 1 charge. This ability cannot be used again until the next dawn.

If you succeed on a saving throw against an enchantment spell targeting only you (with or without the staff's intervention), you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and turn the spell back on its caster as if you had cast the spell.

entries: The staff regains 1d8 + 2 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff becomes a nonmagical quarterstaff.

entries.name: Staff of Fire
entries.description: While holding this staff, you have resistance to fire damage. The staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: {@spell burning hands|PHB} (1 charge), {@spell fireball|PHB} (3 charges), or {@spell wall of fire|PHB} (4 charges). The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff blackens, crumbles into cinders, and is destroyed.

charges.destruction_condition: On a 1 when expending last charge

entries.name: Staff of Frost
entries: You have resistance to cold damage while you hold this staff.

The staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: {@spell cone of cold|DMG} (5 charges), {@spell fog cloud|DMG} (1 charge), {@spell ice storm|DMG} (4 charges), or {@spell wall of ice|DMG} (4 charges).
The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn.
If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff turns to water and is destroyed.

entries.name: Staff of Healing
entries.description: This staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC and spellcasting ability modifier: {@spell cure wounds} (1 charge per spell level, up to 4th), {@spell lesser restoration} (2 charges), or {@spell mass cure wounds} (5 charges). The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff vanishes in a flash of light, lost forever.

entries.name: Staff of Power
entries.description: This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. While holding it, you gain a +2 bonus to AC saving throws and spell attack rolls.
