last_charge_consequences.on_expend: Roll a d20. On a 1, the staff retains its +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls but loses all other properties. On a 20, the staff regains 1d8 + 2 charges.

abilities.name: Power Strike
abilities.description: When you hit with a melee attack using the staff, you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra 1d6 force damage to the target.

abilities.name: Spells
abilities.description: While holding this staff, you can use an action to expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC and spell attack bonus: cone of cold (5 charges), fireball (5th-level version, 5 charges), globe of invulnerability (6 charges), hold monster (5 charges), levitate (2 charges), lightning bolt (5th-level version, 5 charges), magic missile (1 charge), ray of enfeeblement (1 charge), or wall of force (5 charges).

abilities.name: Retributive Strike
abilities.description: You can use an action to break the staff over your knee or against a solid surface, performing a retributive strike. The staff is destroyed and releases its remaining magic in an explosion that expands to fill a 30-foot-radius sphere centered on it. You have a 50% chance to instantly travel to a random plane of existence, avoiding the explosion. If you fail to avoid the effect, you take force damage equal to 16 × the number of charges in the staff. Every other creature in the area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage based on how far away it is from the point of origin (refer to the damage table). On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage.

entries.name: Description
entries: This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it.
The staff has 10 charges. When you hit with a melee attack using it, you can expend up to 3 of its charges. For each charge you expend, the target takes an extra 1d6 force damage.
The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff becomes a nonmagical quarterstaff.

entries.name: Recharge
items: On expending last charge: 1d20 (critical failure makes staff nonmagical)

entries.name: Staff of Swarming Insects
entries.description: This staff has 10 charges and regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, a swarm of insects consumes and destroys the staff, then disperses.
special: If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, a swarm of insects consumes and destroys the staff, then disperses.

abilities.name: Insect Cloud
abilities.description: While holding the staff, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cause a swarm of harmless flying insects to spread out in a 30-foot radius from you. The insects remain for 10 minutes, making the area heavily obscured for creatures other than you. The swarm moves with you, remaining centered on you. A wind of at least 10 miles per hour disperses the swarm and ends the effect.

entries.name: Staff of the Adder

properties.name: Command Word Transformation
entries: Use a bonus action to speak the staff's command word, transforming the head into an animate poisonous snake for 1 minute.
Use another bonus action to speak the command word again, reverting the staff to its inanimate form.

properties.name: Snake Head Attack
entries: Make a melee attack using the snake head (reach 5 feet). Your proficiency bonus applies to the attack roll.
On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d6} piercing damage and must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 3d6} poison damage.

properties.name: Snake Head Statistics
entries: The snake head has an {@ac 15} and {@hp 20} when animate.
If the head drops to 0 hit points, the staff is destroyed. Otherwise, it regains all lost hit points when reverting to its inanimate form.

entries.description: A staff that can transform its head into an animate poisonous snake via a command word, granting temporary melee attacks and poison damage. The snake head can be attacked and has specific hit points and armor class.

entries.name: Staff of the Magi
entries.description: This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. While you hold it, you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls.

properties.name: Charges
properties.value: 50 charges. Regains 4d6 + 2 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 20, the staff regains 1d12 + 1 charges.

properties.name: Spell Absorption
properties.value: While holding the staff, you have advantage on saving throws against spells. You can use your reaction when another creature casts a spell that targets only you. The staff absorbs the magic of the spell, canceling its effect and gaining a number of charges equal to the absorbed spell's level. If this brings the staff's total charges above 50, it explodes as if you activated its retributive strike.

properties.name: Spells
properties.value: While holding the staff, you can use an action to expend charges to cast: conjure elemental (7), dispel magic (3), fireball (7th-level, 7), flaming sphere (2), ice storm (4), invisibility (2), knock (2), lightning bolt (7th-level, 7), passwall (5), plane shift (7), telekinesis (5), wall of fire (4), or web (2). You can also cast arcane lock, detect magic, enlarge/reduce, light, mage hand, or protection from evil and good without using charges.

properties.name: Retributive Strike
properties.value: Use an action to break the staff, destroying it and releasing magic in a 30-foot-radius explosion. You have a 50% chance to teleport to a random plane. Creatures in the area take force damage equal to 16 × charges. On a failed DC 17 Dexterity save, damage varies by distance from the origin.

entries.name: Staff of the Python
entries.description: You can use an action to speak this staff's command word and throw the staff on the ground within 10 feet of you. The staff becomes a giant constrictor snake (see the Monster Manual for statistics) under your control and acts on its own initiative count. By using a bonus action to speak the command word again, you return the staff to its normal form in a space formerly occupied by the snake.
entries.usage: On your turn, you can mentally command the snake if it is within 60 feet of you and you aren't incapacitated. You decide what action the snake takes and where it moves during its next turn, or you can issue it a general command, such as to attack your enemies or guard a location.

properties.attunement_requirement: Requires attunement by a cleric, druid, or warlock
properties.transformation: Transforms into a giant constrictor snake (Monster Manual) on command
properties.revert_condition: Returns to staff form when command word is spoken again (bonus action)

entries.rule: If the snake is reduced to 0 hit points, it dies and reverts to its staff form. The staff then shatters and is destroyed. If the snake reverts to staff form before losing all its hit points, it regains all of them.

entries.name: Description
entries: This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. While holding it, you have a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls.

entries.name: Charges
entries: The staff has 10 charges for the following properties. It regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff loses its properties and becomes a nonmagical quarterstaff.

entries.name: Spells
entries: You can use an action to expend 1 or more of the staff's charges to cast one of the following spells from it, using your spell save DC: {@item animal friendship|DMG} (1 charge), {@item awaken|DMG} (5 charges), {@item barkskin|DMG} (2 charges), {@item locate animals or plants|DMG} (2 charges), {@item speak with animals|DMG} (1 charge), {@item speak with plants|DMG} (3 charges), or {@item wall of thorns|DMG} (6 charges).
You can also use an action to cast the {@item pass without trace|PHB} spell from the staff without using any charges.

entries.name: Tree Form
entries: You can use an action to plant one end of the staff in fertile earth and expend 1 charge to transform the staff into a healthy tree. The tree is 60 feet tall and has a 5-foot-diameter trunk, and its branches at the top spread out in a 20-foot radius. The tree appears ordinary but radiates a faint aura of transmutation magic if targeted by detect magic. While touching the tree and using another action to speak its command, word, you return the staff to its normal form. Any creature in the tree falls when it reverts to a staff.

properties.name: Magic Quarterstaff
properties.description: This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it.

properties.name: Lightning
properties.description: When you hit with a melee attack using the staff, you can cause the target to take an extra {@damage 2d6} lightning damage.

properties.name: Thunder
properties.description: When you hit with a melee attack using the staff, you can cause the staff to emit a crack of thunder, audible out to 300 feet. The target you hit must succeed on a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw or become {@condition prone} until the end of your next turn.

properties.name: Lightning Strike
properties.description: You can use an action to cause a bolt of lightning to leap from the staff's tip in a line that is 5 feet wide and 120 feet long. Each creature in that line must make a {@dc 17} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 9d6} lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

properties.name: Thunderclap
properties.description: You can use an action to cause the staff to issue a deafening thunderclap, audible out to 600 feet. Each creature within 60 feet of you (not including you) must make a {@dc 17} Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 2d6} thunder damage and becomes {@condition deafened} for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half damage and isn't deafened.

properties.name: Thunder and Lightning
properties.description: You can use an action to use the Lightning Strike and Thunderclap properties at the same time. Doing so doesn't expend the daily use of those properties, only the use of this one.

limitations.content: When one of these properties is used, it can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Staff of Withering
entries.description: This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff. On a hit, it deals damage as a normal quarterstaff, and you can expend 1 charge to deal an extra {@damage 2d10} necrotic damage to the target. In addition, the target must succeed on a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw or have disadvantage for 1 hour on any ability check or saving throw that uses Strength or Constitution.

entries.name: Description
entries: When the stone is touching the ground, you can use an action to speak its command word and summon an earth elemental, as if you had cast the {@spell conjure-elemental|PHB} spell. The stone cannot be used this way again until the next dawn. The stone weighs 5 pounds.

entries.name: Stone of Good Luck (Luckstone)
entries.description: While this polished agate is on your person, you gain a +1 bonus to ability checks and saving throws.

entries.name: Sun Blade
entries: Weapon (longsword), rare (requires attunement)

This item appears to be a longsword hilt. While grasping the hilt, you can use a bonus action to cause a blade of pure radiance to spring into existence, or make the blade disappear. While the blade exists, this magic longsword has the finesse property. If you are proficient with shortswords or longswords, you are proficient with the sun blade.

entries.name: Properties
entries: Radiant Damage: The sun blade deals radiant damage instead of slashing damage.
Extra Radiant Damage vs Undead: When you hit an undead with it, that target takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

entries.name: Light Effects
entries: Bright Light: The sword's luminous blade emits bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet. The light is sunlight.
Adjustable Radius: While the blade persists, you can use an action to expand or reduce its radius of bright and dim light by 5 feet each, to a maximum of 30 feet each or a minimum of 10 feet each.

entries.name: Sword of Answering
entries.description: Requires attunement by a creature with the same alignment as the sword. In the world of Greyhawk, only nine of these blades are known to exist. Each is patterned after the legendary sword Fragarach, which is variously translated as "Final Word." Each of the nine swords has its own name and alignment, and each bears a different gem in its pommel.

abilities.name: Attack and Damage Bonus
abilities.description: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this sword.

abilities.name: Defensive Strike
abilities.description: While you hold the sword, you can use your reaction to make one melee attack with it against any creature in your reach that deals damage to you. You have advantage on the attack roll, and any damage dealt with this special attack ignores any damage immunity or resistance the target has.

lore.name: Sword of Answering Lore
lore.description: The Sword of Answering is one of nine legendary blades from the world of Greyhawk, each patterned after Fragarach ("Final Word"). These swords are aligned with specific alignments and feature unique gems in their pommels. They are attuned to creatures of matching alignment and grant powerful defensive capabilities.

entries.name: Sword of Life Stealing
entries.description: Requires attunement. When you attack a creature with this magic weapon, if you roll a 20 on the attack roll, that target takes an extra {@damage necrotic} damage if it isn't a construct or an undead. You also gain 10 temporary hit points.

entries.name: SWORD OF SHARPNESS
entries.description: When you attack an object with this magic sword and hit, maximize your weapon damage dice against the target. When you attack a creature with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, that target takes an extra {@damage 1d8} slashing damage. Then roll another d20. If you roll a 20, you lop off one of the target's limbs, with the effect of such loss determined by the DM. If the creature has no limbs to sever, you lop off a portion of its body instead. In addition, you can speak the sword's command to cause the blade to shed bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. Speaking the command word again or sheathing the sword puts out the light.

properties.name: +1 bonus
properties.value: attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon

properties.name: Cursed Spirit
properties.value: Becoming attuned to this weapon extends the curse to you. You are unwilling to part with the sword, keeping it on your person at all times. While attuned to this weapon, you have disadvantage on attack rolls made with weapons other than this one.

properties.name: Compulsion on Damage
properties.value: While the sword is on your person, you must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw whenever you take damage in combat. On a failed save, you must attack the creature that damaged you until it drops to 0 hit points or you can't reach the creature to make a melee attack against it.

properties.name: Breaking the Curse
properties.value: The curse can be broken by usual methods. Casting banishment on the sword forces the vengeful spirit to leave it, reducing it to a +1 weapon with no other properties.

entries.name: Sword of Wounding
entries.description: Hit points lost to this weapon's damage can be regained only through a short or long rest, rather than by regeneration, magic, or any other means.

entries.name: Wound Effect
entries: Once per turn, when you hit a creature with an attack using this magic weapon, you can wound the target.

items: At the start of each of the wounded creature's turns, it takes {@damage 1d4 necrotic} damage for each time you've wounded it.
The wounded creature can make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a success, the effect of all such wounds ends immediately.

entries.name: Alternative Ending
entries: Alternatively, the wounded creature, or a creature within 5 feet of it, can use an action to make a {@dc 15} Wisdom (Medicine) check. On a success, the effect of such wounds ends.

entries.name: Talisman of Pure Good
entries.attunement: requires attunement by a creature of good alignment
entries.description:
This talisman is a mighty symbol of goodness. A creature that is neither good nor evil in alignment takes {@damage 6d6} radiant damage upon touching the talisman. An evil creature takes {@damage 8d6} radiant damage upon touching the talisman. Either sort of creature takes the damage again each time it ends its turn holding or carrying the talisman.

If you are a good cleric or paladin, you can use the talisman as a holy symbol, and you gain a +2 bonus to {@attack roll} while you wear or hold it.

charges.usage: When wearing or holding the talisman, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and choose one creature you can see on the ground within 120 feet. If the target is of evil alignment, a flaming fissure opens under it. The target must succeed on a {@dc 20} Dexterity saving throw or fall into the fissure and be destroyed, leaving no remains. The fissure then closes, leaving no trace of its existence. When you expend the last charge, the talisman disperses into motes of golden light and is destroyed.

entries.content: When you make an Intelligence (Arcana) check to control a sphere of annihilation while holding this talisman, you double your proficiency bonus on the check.
When you start your turn with control over a sphere of annihilation, you can use an action to levitate it 10 feet plus a number of additional feet equal to 10 × your Intelligence modifier.

entries.name: TALISMAN OF ULTIMATE EVIL
entries.attunement: requires attunement by a creature of evil alignment
entries.description: A creature that is neither good nor evil in alignment takes 6d6 necrotic damage upon touching the talisman. A good creature takes 8d6 necrotic damage upon touching the talisman. Either sort of creature takes the damage again each time it ends its turn holding or carrying the talisman.

special_abilities.name: Holy Symbol Bonus
entries: If you are an evil cleric or paladin, you can use the talisman as a holy symbol, and you gain a +2 bonus to spell attack rolls while you wear or hold it.

special_abilities.name: Fissure Spell
entries: You can use an action to expend 1 charge from the talisman and choose one creature you can see on the ground within 120 feet of you. If the target is of good alignment, a flaming fissure opens under it. The target must succeed on a DC 20 Dexterity saving throw or fall into the fissure and be destroyed, leaving no remains. The fissure then closes, leaving no trace of its existence.

special_abilities.name: Destruction
entries: When you expend the last charge, the talisman dissolves into foul-smelling slime and is destroyed.

entries.name: Tentacle Rod
entries.description: Made by the drow, this rod is a magic weapon that ends in three rubbery tentacles. While holding the rod, you can use an action to direct each tentacle to attack a creature you can see within 15 feet of you. Each tentacle makes a melee attack roll with a +9 bonus. On a hit, the tentacle deals {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage. If you hit a target with all three tentacles, it must make a {@dc 15} Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the creature's speed is halved, it has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws, and it can't use reactions for 1 minute. Moreover, on each of its turns, it can take either an action or a bonus action, but not both. At the end of each of its turns, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

effects.name: Disadvantage on Dexterity Saving Throws
effects.description: The creature has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

effects.name: Cannot Use Reactions
effects.description: The creature can't use reactions for 1 minute.

effects.name: Action/Bonus Action Restriction
effects.description: On each of its turns, the creature can take either an action or a bonus action, but not both.

entities.name: TOME OF CLEAR THOUGHT Wondrous item, very rare

entries.text: This book contains memory and logic exercises, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Intelligence score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The manual then loses its magic, but regains it after a century.

entities.name: TOME OF LEADERSHIP AND INFLUENCE Wondrous item, very rare

entries.name: TOME OF LEADERSHIP AND INFLUENCE
entries.description: This book contains guidelines for influencing and charming others, and its words are charged with magic. If you spend 48 hours over a period of 6 days or fewer studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines, your Charisma score increases by 2, as your maximum for that score. The manual then loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

entities.name: TOME OF THE STILLED TONGUE wizard)

entries.name: Tome of the Stilled Tongue
entries.lore: This thick leather-bound volume has a desiccated tongue pinned to the front cover. Five of these tomes exist, and it's unknown which one is the original. The grisly cover decoration on the first tome of the stilled tongue once belonged to a treacherous former servant of the lich-god Vecna, keeper of secrets. The tongues pinned to the covers of the four copies came from other spellcasters who crossed Vecna. The first few pages of each tome are filled with indecipherable scrawls. The remaining pages are blank and pristine.
mechanics: If you can attune to this item, you can use it as a spellbook and an arcane focus.

While holding the tome, you can use a bonus action to cast a spell you have written in this tome, without expending a spell slot or using any verbal or somatic component. Once used, this property of the tome can't be used again until the next dawn.
consequences: While attuned to the book, you can remove the tongue from the book's cover. If you do so, all spells written in the book are permanently erased.
special_abilities: Vecna watches anyone using this tome. He can also send cryptic messages in the book. These messages appear at midnight and fade away after they are read.

entities.name: TOME OF UNDERSTANDING Tondrous item, very rare

entries.name: Tome of Understanding
entries.description: If you spend 48 hours studying the book's contents and practicing its guidelines during a period of 6 days or fewer, your Wisdom score increases by 2, as does your maximum for that score. The manual then loses its magic, but regains it in a century.

entries.name: Description
entries: This trident is a magic weapon. It has 3 charges. While you carry it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to cast {@spell Dominate Beast|PHB} (save DC 15) from it on a beast that has an innate swimming speed. The trident regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Universal Solvent
entries.description: This tube holds milky liquid with a strong alcohol melt. You can use an action to pour the contents of the tube onto a surface within reach. The liquid instantly dissolves up to 1 square foot of adhesive it touches, including sovereign glue.

entries.name: VICIOUS WEAPON
entries.description: When you roll a 20 on your attack roll with this magic weapon, the target takes an extra 7 damage of the weapon's type.

entries.name: Description
entries: Weapon (any sword that deals slashing damage), legendary (requires attunement). You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores resistance to slashing damage.

entries.name: Special Ability
entries: When you attack a creature that has at least one head with this weapon and roll a 20 on the attack roll, you cut off one of the creature's heads. The creature dies if it can't survive without the lost head.

A creature is immune to this effect if it is immune to slashing damage, doesn't have or need a head, has legendary actions, or {@dm} decides that the creature is too large for its head to be cut off with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes an extra {@damage 6d8} slashing damage from the hit.

entries.name: Wand of Binding
entries.description: This wand has 7 charges for the following properties. It regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

properties.name: Spells
properties.description: While holding the wand, you can use an action to expend some of its charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 17): hold monster (5 charges) or hold person (2 charges).

properties.name: Assisted Escape
properties.description: While holding the wand, you can use your reaction to expend 1 charge and gain advantage on a saving throw you make to avoid being paralyzed or restrained, or you can expend 1 charge and gain advantage on any check you make to escape a grapple.

destruction_condition.description: On a 1 during the final charge use, the wand is destroyed.

entries.name: Wand of Enemy Detection
entries.description: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action and expend 1 charge to speak its command word. For the next minute, you know the direction of the nearest creature hostile to you within 60 feet, but not its distance from you. The wand can sense the presence of hostile creatures that are ethereal, invisible, disguised, or hidden, as well as those in plain sight. The effect ends if you stop holding the wand. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

charges.special: If the last charge is expended, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand is destroyed.

entries.name: Wand of Fear
entries: A rare wand that requires attunement. It has 7 charges and regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

entries.name: Command
entries: Use an action to expend 1 charge and command another creature to flee or grovel (as the *command* spell). The target must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or be affected.

entries.name: Cone of Fear
entries: Use an action to expend 2 charges, causing the wand's tip to emit a 60-foot cone of amber light. Each creature in the cone must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened of you for 1 minute.

While frightened in this way, a creature must spend its turns trying to move as far away from you as it can. It can willingly move to a space within 30 feet of you, but cannot take reactions. For its action, it can only use the Dash action or attempt to escape from an effect preventing movement. If it has nowhere to move, it can use the Dodge action.
At the end of each of its turns, a creature can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Wand of Fireballs
entries.description: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast the {@spell fireball} spell (save DC {@dc 15}) from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 3rd-level version of the spell. You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend. The wand regains {@recharge 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

entries.name: Description
entries: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast the {@spell lightning bolt} spell (save DC 15) from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 3rd-level version of the spell. You can raise the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend.
The wand regains {@recharge ld6 + 1} charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

entries.name: Properties
items: Attunement: requires attunement by a {@spellcasting_ability spellcaster}

entries.name: And of Magic Detection
entries.description: The wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can expend 1 charge as an action to cast the detect magic spell from it. The wand regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Magic Missiles
entries.description: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 or more of its charges to cast the magic missile spell from it. For 1 charge, you cast the 1st-level version of the spell. You can increase the spell slot level by one for each additional charge you expend. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

entries.name: Wand of Paralysis
entries.description: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cause a thin blue ray to streak from the tip toward a creature you can see within 60 feet. Make a ranged attack roll using your spell attack bonus. On a hit, the target is paralyzed for 1 minute. At the end of each of the target's turns, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

entries.name: Wand of Polymorph
entries: A very rare wand (requires attunement by a spellcaster) with 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast {@spell polymorph|PHB} (save DC {@dc 15}) from it.
The wand regains {@dice 1d6 + 1} expended charges daily at dawn.
If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

entries.name: Wand of Secrets
entries.description: While holding the wand, you can use an action to expend 1 charge. If a secret door or trap is within 30 feet of you, the wand pulses and points at the one nearest to you. The wand regains 1d3 expended charges daily at dawn.

entities.name: WAND OF THE WAR MAGE, +1, +2 , OR +3 Wand,  uncommon (+1), rare (+2), or very rare (+3)

entries.name: Wand of the War Mage
entries: A wand with +1, +2, or +3 enhancement bonuses (uncommon, rare, or very rare).
Requires attunement by a spellcaster.
While holding this wand, you gain a bonus to spell attack rolls determined by the wand's rarity.
You ignore half cover when making a spell attack.

entities.name: Wand,  uncommon (requires attunement by a spellcaster)

entries.name: Wand
entries.description: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast {@item Web|PHB.spell.Web} (save DC 15) from it. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into ashes and is destroyed.

entries.name: Wand of Wonder
entries.description: While holding this wand, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and choose a target within 120 feet. Roll 1d100 to determine the effect. If the effect causes you to cast a spell from the wand, the spell's save DC is 15. If the spell normally has a range expressed in feet, its range becomes 120 feet if it isn't already. If an effect covers an area, you must center the spell on and include the target. If an effect has multiple possible subjects, the DM randomly determines which ones are affected. If you expend the wand's last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand crumbles into dust and is destroyed.

effects.description: Heavy rain falls in a 60-foot radius centered on the target. The area becomes lightly obscured. The rain falls until the start of your next turn.

An animal appears in the unoccupied space nearest the target. The animal isn't under your control and acts as it normally would. Roll a d10 to determine which animal appears: 01-25 (rhinoceros), 26-50 (elephant), 51-100 (rat). See {@item Monster Manual|MM} for the animal's statistics.
A cloud of 600 oversized butterflies fills a 30-foot radius centered on the target. The area becomes heavily obscured. The butterflies remain for 10 minutes.
You enlarge the target as if you had cast enlarge/reduce. If the target can't be affected by that spell or if you didn't target a creature, you become the target.

items: You have a bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. The bonus is determined by the weapon's rarity.

entries.description: While the weapon is on your person, you have {@advantage initiative}. You and any companions within 30 feet can't be surprised, except when incapacitated by something other than nonmagical sleep. The weapon magically awakens you and your companions within range if any of you are sleeping naturally when combat begins.

entries.name: Description
entries: While you wear these boots, you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed. You can use the boots to fly for up to 4 hours, all at once or in several shorter flights, each one using a minimum of 1 minute from the duration. If you are flying when the duration expires, you descend at a rate of 30 feet per round until you land.
The boots regain 2 hours of flying capability for every 12 hours they aren't in use.

entries.name: Description
entries: While wearing this cloak, you can use an action to speak its command word. This turns the cloak into a pair of bat wings or bird wings on your back for 1 hour or until you repeat the command word as an action. The wings give you a flying speed of 60 feet. When they disappear, you can't use them again for 1d12 hours.

entries.name: Sentient Magic Items
entries: Sentient magic items possess personality and behave like NPCs under the DM's control. They may have quirks, ideals, bonds, and flaws, and can be allies or adversaries to their wielders.
Most sentient items are weapons, though other item types (excluding consumables) may manifest sentience. Potions, scrolls, and other consumable items are never sentient.

Activated properties of sentient items are controlled by the item itself, not the wielder. A good relationship allows normal access to these properties; strained relationships may cause the item to suppress or oppose its abilities.

entries.name: Creating Sentient Magic Items
entries.description: When crafting a sentient magic item, create its persona as an NPC with exceptions outlined in the text.

entries.name: Communication
entries: A sentient item has some ability to communicate, either by sharing its emotions, broadcasting its thoughts telepathically, or speaking aloud. You can choose how it communicates or roll on the following table.
The table referenced here is not included in the provided text.

entries.name: Emotion Transmission
entries.description: The item communicates by transmitting emotion to the creature carrying or wielding it.

entries.name: Language Understanding
entries.description: The item can speak, read, and understand one or more languages.

entries.name: Telepathic Communication
entries.description: The item can speak, read, and understand one or more languages. In addition, the item can communicate telepathically with any character that carries or wields it.

entries.name: Sentient Item Awareness
entries: A sentient item can perceive its surroundings out to a limited range.
You can choose its senses or roll on the following table (table not provided in source text).
Hearing and normal vision out to 30 feet.
Hearing and normal vision out to 60 feet
Hearing and normal vision out to 120 feet.
Hearing and darkvision out to 120 feet.

entries.name: Sentient Magic Item Alignment
entries.description: A sentient magic item has an alignment. Its creator or nature might suggest an alignment. If not, you can pick an alignment or roll on the following table.
rules: A sentient magic item's alignment is determined by its creator, its nature, or a roll on a table (referenced but not detailed here).

entries.name: Creating Sentient Items
entries: Use the information on creating NPCs in chapter 4 to develop a sentient item's mannerisms, personality traits, ideals, bonds, and flaws. You can also draw on the "Special Features" section earlier in this chapter.
If you determine these characteristics randomly, ignore or adapt any result that doesn't make sense for an inanimate object. You can reroll until you get a result you like.

entries.name: Special Purpose
entries.description: You can give a sentient item an objective it pursues, perhaps to the exclusion of all else. As long as the wielder's use of the item aligns with that special purpose, the item remains cooperative. Deviating from this course might cause conflict between the wielder and the item, and could even cause the item to prevent the use of its activated properties. You can pick a special purpose or roll on the following table.

entries.name: Aligned
entries.description: The item seeks to defeat or destroy those of a diametrically opposed alignment. (Such an item is never neutral.)

entries.name: Bane
entries.description: The item seeks to defeat or destroy creatures of a particular kind, such as fiends, shapechangers, trolls, or wizards.

entries.name: Protector
entries.description: The item seeks to defend a particular race or kind of creature, such as elves or druids.

entries.name: Crusader
entries.description: The item seeks to defeat, weaken, or destroy the servants of a particular deity.

entries.name: Templar
entries.description: The item seeks to defend the servants and interests of a particular deity.

entries.name: Destroyer
entries.description: The item craves destruction and goads its user to fight arbitrarily.

entries.name: Glory Seeker
entries.description: The item seeks renown as the greatest magic item in the world, by establishing its user as a famous or notorious figure.

entries.name: Lore Seeker
entries.description: The item craves knowledge or is determined to solve a mystery, learn a secret, or unravel a cryptic prophecy.

entries.name: Destiny Seeker
entries.description: The item is convinced that it and its wielder have key roles to play in future events.

entries.name: Creator Seeker
entries.description: The item seeks its creator and wants to understand why it was created.

entries.name: Conflict Resolution
entries: When a sentient item's alignment or purpose is opposed by its wielder, the item makes a Charisma check contested by the wielder's Charisma check.
If the item wins the contest, it may demand one or more of the following:

items: The item insists on being carried or worn at all times.
The item demands that its wielder dispose of anything the item finds repugnant.
The item demands that its wielder pursue the item's goals to the exclusion of all other goals.
The item demands to be given to someone else.

entries.name: Consequences of Refusal
entries: If the wielder refuses to comply, the item can do any or all of the following:

items: Make it impossible for its wielder to attune to it.
Suppress one or more of its activated properties.
Attempt to take control of its wielder.

entries.name: Take Control Mechanics
entries: The wielder must make a Charisma saving throw {@dc 12 + the item's Charisma modifier}.
On a failed save, the wielder is charmed by the item for 1d12 hours.
While charmed, the wielder must try to follow the item's commands.
If the wielder takes damage, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
The item cannot use this power again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Blackrazor
entries.description: Hidden in the dungeon of White Plume Mountain, Blackrazor shines like a piece of night sky filled with stars. Its black scabbard is decorated with pieces of cut obsidian.

entries.name: Attack and Damage Bonus
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Soul Devouring
entries: Whenever you use it to reduce a creature to 0 hit points, the sword slays the creature and devours its soul, unless it is a construct or an undead.
A creature whose soul has been devoured by Blackrazor can be restored to life only by a wish spell.

entries.name: Temporary Hit Points
entries: When it devours a soul, Blackrazor grants you temporary hit points equal to the slain creature's hit point maximum. These hit points fade after 24 hours.

entries.name: Advantage on Rolls
entries: As long as these temporary hit points last and you keep Blackrazor in hand, you have advantage on attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks.

entries.name: Necrotic Damage vs Undead
entries: If you hit an undead with this weapon, you take 1d10 necrotic damage and the target regains 1d10 hit points.
If this necrotic damage reduces you to 0 hit points, Blackrazor devours your soul.

entries.name: Soul Detection
entries: While you hold the weapon, you are aware of the presence of Tiny or larger creatures within 60 feet of you that aren't constructs or undead.

entries.name: Resist Charm and Fear
entries: You also can't be charmed or frightened.

entries.name: Haste Spell
entries: Blackrazor can cast the haste spell on you once per day. It decides when to cast the spell and maintains concentration on it so that you don't have to.

entries.name: Intelligence, Wisdom, Charisma
entries: Blackrazor is a sentient chaotic neutral weapon with an Intelligence of 17, a Wisdom of 10, and a Charisma of 19.

entries.name: Senses
entries: It has hearing and darkvision out to a range of 120 feet.

entries.name: Language Understanding
entries: The weapon can speak, read, and understand Common, and can communicate with its wielder telepathically. Its voice is deep and echoing.
While you are attuned to it, Blackrazor also understands every language you know.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Blackrazor speaks with an imperious tone, as though accustomed to being obeyed.
The sword's purpose is to consume souls. It doesn't care whose souls it eats, including the wielder's.
The weapon believes that all matter and energy sprang from a void of negative energy and will one day return to it. Blackrazor is meant to hurry that process along.

Despite its nihilism, Blackrazor feels a strange kinship to Wave and Whelm, two other weapons locked away under White Plume Mountain. It wants the three weapons to be united again and wielded together in combat, even though it violently disagrees with Whelm and finds Wave tedious.

entries.name: Soul Hunger Conflict
entries: Blackrazor's hunger for souls must be regularly fed. If the sword goes three days or more without consuming a soul, a conflict between it and its wielder occurs at the next sunset.

entries.name: Moonblade
entries.attunement: requires attunement by an elf or half-elf of neutral good alignment
entries.description: A moonblade passes down from parent to child, bonding with its bearer for life. If the bearer dies, another heir may claim it; otherwise, it lies dormant and functions like a normal longsword until found by a worthy soul. The blade serves only one master at a time, requiring a ritual attunement in an elven regent's throne room or an elven temple. It rejects craven, erratic, corrupt, or anti-elvenkind users, granting disadvantage on ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws for 24 hours if rejected. Acceptance grants attunement and a new rune on the blade.

properties.name: Rune Mechanics
entries: The first rune grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls with the weapon.
Each additional rune (1d6 + 1 total) grants a property chosen by the DM from the Moon Blade Properties table.

items: Dormant state without a worthy heir

entries.content: Sentient neutral good weapon with Intelligence 12, Wisdom 10, Charisma 12. Has hearing and darkvision out to 120 feet. Communicates via tingling sensations or visions/dreams when the wielder is in a trance or asleep.

Seeks advancement of elvenkind and ideals (courage, loyalty, beauty, music, life). Bonded to family line; loyalty is absolute once bonded to an owner with shared ideals. Flaw: overconfidence, believing only its chosen owner should wield it.

entries.name: Description
entries: Held in the dungeon of White Plume Mountain, this trident is an exquisite weapon engraved with images of waves, shells, and sea creatures. Although you must worship a god of the sea to attune to this weapon, Wave happily accepts new converts.

entries.name: Properties
entries: Bonus: +3 to attack and damage rolls with this weapon.
Critical Hit: On a critical hit, the target takes extra necrotic damage equal to half its hit point maximum.
Functions: Acts as a trident of fish command and a weapon of warning. Confers the benefit of a cap of water breathing while held, and can be used as a cube of force by choosing its effect.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: Intelligence: 14, Wisdom: 10, Charisma: 18
Senses: Hearing and darkvision out to 120 feet
Communication: Telepathic with wielder. Speaks, reads, and understands Aquan. Can speak with aquatic animals via telepathy.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Humming tunes (sea shanties or hymns of sea gods) when restless.
Zealously seeks to convert mortals to sea god worship or consign the faithless to death.
Conflict arises if wielder fails to advance its objectives.

entries.name: Lore
entries: Nostalgic attachment to Thunderforge, a desolate island where a sea god imprisoned storm giants. The giants forged Wave in an act of devotion or rebellion against the god.
Secret doubt about its purpose: Fears it was created to destroy a specific sea god, a destiny it may not avoid.

entries.name: Description
entries: Whelm is a legendary warhammer forged by dwarves and lost in the dungeon of White Plume Mountain. It requires attunement by a dwarf to use.
You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

entries.name: Effects
entries: At dawn the day after you first make an attack roll with Whelm, you develop a fear of being outdoors that persists as long as you remain attuned to the weapon. This causes you to have disadvantage on attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks while you can see the daytime sky.

items: Thrown Weapon: Whelm has the thrown property, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet. When you hit with a ranged weapon attack using it, the target takes an extra 1d8 bludgeoning damage, or an extra 2d8 bludgeoning damage if the target is a giant. Each time you throw the weapon, it flies back to your hand after the attack. If you don't have a hand free, the weapon lands at your feet.

entries.name: Shock Wave
entries: You can use an action to strike the ground with Whelm and send a shock wave out from the point of impact. Each creature of your choice on the ground within 60 feet of that point must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become stunned for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Supernatural Awareness
entries: While you are holding the weapon, it alerts you to the location of any secret or concealed doors within 30 feet of you. In addition, you can use an action to cast detect evil and good or locate object from the weapon. Once you cast either spell, you can't cast it from the weapon again until the next dawn.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: Whelm is a sentient lawful neutral weapon with an Intelligence of 15, a Wisdom of 12, and a Charisma of 15. It has hearing and darkvision out to a range of 120 feet. The weapon communicates telepathically with its wielder and can speak, read, and understand Dwarvish, Giant, and Goblin. It shouts battle cries in Dwarvish when used in combat.

entries.name: Personality & Lore
entries: Whelm's purpose is to slaughter giants and goblinoids. It also seeks to protect dwarves against all enemies. Conflict arises if the wielder fails to destroy goblins and giants or to protect dwarves.
Whelm has ties to the dwarf clan that created it, variously called the Dankil or the Mightyhammer clan. It longs to be returned to that clan. It would do anything to protect those dwarves from harm.

The hammer also carries a secret shame. Centuries ago, a dwarf named Ctenmiir wielded it valiantly for a time. But Ctenmiir was turned into a vampire. His will was strong enough that he bent Whelm to his evil purposes, even killing members of his own clan.

An artifact is a unique magic item of tremendous power, with its own origin and history. An artifact might have been created by gods or mortals of awesome power. It could have been created in the midst of a crisis that threatened a kingdom, a world, or the entire multiverse, and carry the weight of that pivotal moment in history.

Some artifacts appear when they are needed most. For others, the reverse is true; when discovered, the world trembles at the ramifications of the find. In either case, introducing an artifact into a campaign requires forethought. The artifact could be an item that opposing sides are hoping to claim, or it might be something the adventurers need to overcome their greatest challenge.

Characters don't typically find artifacts in the normal course of adventuring. In fact, artifacts only appear when you want them to, for they are as much plot devices as magic items. Tracking down and recovering an artifact is often the main goal of an adventure. Characters must chase down rumors,

entries.name: Property Effects
entries: While attuned to the artifact, you gain proficiency in one skill of the OM's choice.
While attuned to the artifact, you are immune to disease.
While attuned to the artifact, you can't be charmed or frightened.
While attuned to the artifact, you have resistance against one damage type of the OM's choice.
While attuned to the artifact, you can use an action to cast one cantrip (chosen by the OM) from it.

While attuned to the artifact, you can use an action to cast one 1st-level spell (chosen by the OM) from it. After you cast the spell, roll a d6. On a roll of 1-5, you can't cast it again until the next dawn.
As 61-70 above, except the spell is 2nd level.
As 61-70 above, except the spell is 3rd level.
While attuned to the artifact, you gain a +1 bonus to Armor Class.

entries.name: Artifact Properties
entries.description: Artifacts can have up to four minor beneficial properties and two major beneficial properties. They can also have up to four minor detrimental properties and two major detrimental properties. Properties may vary each time an artifact appears in the world.

entries.name: Major Beneficial Properties
entries: The entry appears to reference a table or list of properties, but the source text is incomplete or malformed. The line 'd  1  00 Property' suggests a die roll (possibly d100) and a property name, but lacks sufficient detail to extract meaningful rules data. No further information is provided about the property's function, effects, or usage.

entries.text: While attuned to the artifact, you have disadvantage on saving throws against spells.
The first time you touch a gem or piece of jewelry while attuned to this artifact, the value of the gem or jewelry is reduced by half.
While attuned to the artifact, you are blinded when you are more than 10 feet away from it.
While attuned to the artifact, you have disadvantage on saving throws against poison.
While attuned to the artifact, you emit a sour stench noticeable from up to 10 feet away.
While attuned to the artifact, all holy water within 10 feet of you is destroyed.
While attuned to the artifact, you are physically ill and have disadvantage on any ability check or saving throw that uses Strength or Constitution.
While attuned to the artifact, your weight increases by 1d4 x 10 pounds.
While attuned to the artifact, your appearance changes as the OM decides.
While attuned to the artifact, you are deafened when you are more than 10 feet away from it.
While attuned to the artifact, your weight drops by 1d4 x 5 pounds.
While attuned to the artifact, you can't smell.
While attuned to the artifact, nonmagical flames are extinguished within 30 feet of you.
While you are attuned to the artifact, other creatures can't take short or long rests while within 300 feet of you.
While attuned to the artifact, you deal 1d6 necrotic damage to any plant you touch that isn't a creature.
While you are attuned to the artifact, animals within 30 feet of you are hostile toward you.
While attuned to the artifact, you must eat and drink six times the normal amount each day.
While you are attuned to the artifact, your flaw is amplified in a way determined by the OM.

entries.name: Body Rot Property
entries.description: While you are attuned to the artifact, your body rots over the course of four days, after which the rotting stops. You lose your hair by the end of day 1, finger tips and toe tips by the end of day 2, lips and nose by the end of day 3, and ears by the end of day 4. A {@spell regenerate} spell restores lost body parts.

entries.name: Alignment Determination Property
entries.description: While you are attuned to the artifact, you determine your alignment daily at dawn by rolling a d6 twice. On the first roll, a 1-2 indicates lawful, 3-4 neutral, and 5-6 chaotic. On the second roll, a 1-2 indicates good, 3-4 neutral, and 5-6 evil.

entries.name: Quest Property
entries.description: When you first attune to the artifact, it gives you a quest determined by the {@item OM|DMG}. You must complete this quest as if affected by the {@spell geas} spell. Once you complete the quest, you are no longer affected by this property.

entries.name: Hostile Life Force Property
entries.description: The artifact houses a bodiless life force that is hostile toward you. Each day, you must make a {@dc 20} Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, you take 1d6 necrotic damage and the life force gains advantage on its next attack against you.

entries.name: Corruption Property
entries.description: While you are attuned to the artifact, you gain +1 to your Charisma saving throws against {@spell dispel evil and good}. However, you also gain a curse that causes you to gain 1d6 necrotic damage when you cast a spell.

entries.name: Cursed Transformation Property
entries.description: While you are attuned to the artifact, you gain a curse that causes you to transform into a beast once per day. This transformation lasts for 1 hour and grants you advantage on Strength saving throws but imposes disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

entries.name: Mental Dominion Property
entries.description: While you are attuned to the artifact, you gain a curse that causes you to suffer from a mental affliction. Once per day, you can force a creature within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw against a {@dc 15} spell. On a failure, the target is charmed by you for 1 minute.

entries.name: Soulbound Property
entries.description: While you are attuned to the artifact, you gain a curse that causes you to be bound to the artifact's soul. Once per day, you can force a creature within 30 feet to make a Wisdom saving throw against a {@dc 15} spell. On a failure, the target is bound to the artifact for 1 hour.

entries.name: Ability Score Reduction
entries: When you become attuned to the artifact, one of your ability scores is reduced by 2 at random. A greater restoration spell restores the ability to normal.

entries.name: Aging and Death
entries: Each time you become attuned to the artifact, you age 3d10 years. You must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or die from the shock. If you die, you are instantly transformed into a wight {@item Wight|MonsterManual} under the artifact's control that is sworn to protect the artifact.

entries.name: Loss of Speech
entries: While attuned to the artifact, you lose the ability to speak.

entries.name: Vulnerability to All Damage
entries: While attuned to the artifact, you have vulnerability to all damage.

entries.name: God's Avatar
entries: When you become attuned to the artifact, there is a 10 percent chance that you attract the attention of a god that sends an avatar to wrest the artifact from you. The avatar has the same alignment as its creator and the statistics of an empyrean {@item Empyrean|MonsterManual}. Once it obtains the artifact, the avatar vanishes.

entries.name: Destroying Artifacts
entries.description: An artifact must be destroyed in some special way. Otherwise, it is impervious to damage. Each artifact has a weakness by which its creation can be undone. Learning this weakness might require extensive research or the successful completion of a quest. The DM decides how a particular artifact can be destroyed.
methods: The artifact must be melted down in the volcano, forge, or crucible in which it was created.
The artifact must be dropped into the River Styx.
The artifact must be swallowed and digested by the tarrasque or some other ancient creature.
The artifact must be bathed in the blood of a god or an angel.
The artifact must be struck and shattered by a special weapon crafted for that purpose.
The artifact must be pulverized between the titanic gears of Mechanus.
The artifact must be returned to its creator, who can destroy it by touch.

entities.name: AxE OF THE D WARVISH LORDS

entries.name: Axe of the Dwarvish Lords
entries.description: The Axe of the Dwarvish Lords is a magic weapon that grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. The axe also functions as a belt of dwarvenkind, a dwarven thrower, and a sword of sharpness. [...] Destroying the axe is only possible by melting it in the Earthheart Forge for fifty years.

entries.name: Book of Exalted Deeds
entries.description: The definitive treatise on all that is good in the multiverse, the fabled Book of Exalted Deeds figures prominently in many religions. Rather than being a scripture devoted to a particular faith, the book's various authors filled the pages with their own vision of true virtue, providing guidance for defeating evil. The Book of Exalted Deeds rarely lingers in one place. As soon, the book is read, it vanishes to some other corner of the multiverse where its moral guidance can bring light to a darkened world. Although attempts have been made to copy the work, efforts to do so fail to capture its magical nature or translate the benefits it offers to those pure of heart and firm of purpose.

benefits.name: Wisdom Increase
entries: After you spend the requisite amount of time reading and studying the book, your Wisdom score increases by 2, to a maximum of 24. You can't gain this benefit from the book more than once.

benefits.name: Enlightened Magic
entries: Once you've read and studied the book, any spell slot you expend to cast a cleric or paladin spell counts as a spell slot of one level higher.

benefits.name: Halo
entries: Once you've read and studied the book, you gain a protective halo. This halo sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet. You can dismiss or manifest the halo as a bonus action. While present, the halo gives you advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks made to interact with good creatures and Charisma (Intimidation) checks made to interact with evil creatures. In addition, fiends and undead within the halo's bright light make attack rolls against you with disadvantage.

special_abilities.name: Evil Reader Damage
entries: An evil creature that tries to read from the book takes 24d6 radiant damage. This damage ignores resistance and immunity, and can't be reduced or avoided by any means. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this damage disappears in a blinding flash and is destroyed, leaving its possessions behind.

special_abilities.name: Duration Conditions
entries: Benefits granted by the Book of Exalted Deeds last only as long as you strive to do good. If you fail to perform at least one act of kindness or generosity within the span of 10 days, or if you willingly perform an evil act, you lose all the benefits granted by the book.

special_abilities.name: Destruction Method
entries: It is rumored that the Book of Exalted Deeds can't be destroyed as long as good exists in the multiverse. However, drowning the book in the River Styx removes all writing and imagery from its pages and renders the book powerless for 1d100 years.

entries.name: Description
entries: The contents of this foul manuscript of ineffable wickedness are the meat and drink of those in evil's thrall. No mortal was meant to know the secrets it contains, knowledge so horrid that to even glimpse the scrawled pages invites madness.

Most believe the lich-god Vecna authored the Book of Vile Darkness. He recorded in its pages every disease idea, every unhinged thought, and every example of blackest magic he came across or devised. Vecna covered every vile topic he could, making the book a gruesome catalog of all mortal wrongs.

Other practitioners of evil have held the book and added their own input to its catalog of vile knowledge. Their additions are clear, for the writers of later works stitched whatever they were writing into the tome or, in some cases, made notations and additions to existing text. There are places where pages are missing, torn, or covered so completely with ink, blood, and scratches that the original text can't be divined.

entries.name: Special Properties
entries: Nature can't abide the book's presence. Ordinary plants wither in its presence, animals are unwilling to approach it, and the book gradually destroys whatever it touches. Even stone cracks and turns to powder if the book rests on it long enough.

entries.name: Book of Vile Darkness
entries.description: A cursed tome containing forbidden knowledge and dark magic. It grants its attuned wielder powerful abilities but corrupts them with evil influence.

properties.name: Adjusted Ability Scores
properties.description: After studying the book for 80 hours, one ability score increases by 2 (max 24) and another decreases by 2 (min 3).

properties.name: Mark of Darkness
properties.description: A physical disfigurement grants advantage on Charisma (Persuasion) checks with evil creatures and Charisma (Intimidation) checks with non-evil creatures.

properties.name: Command Evil
properties.description: Once per dawn, cast dominate monster on an evil target (DC 18 save).

properties.name: Dark Lore
properties.description: Double proficiency bonus on Intelligence checks to recall evil-related lore (e.g., demons).

properties.name: Dark Speech
properties.description: As an action, recite dark speech causing 1d12 psychic damage to self and 3d6 to non-evil creatures within 15 feet.

properties.name: Destroying the Book
properties.description: Torn pages reform after 1d100 years; solar tearing destroys it for 1d100 years. A 100-year attunement reveals a phrase that destroys both the speaker and the book when spoken in Celestial. If all evil is erased, the book turns to dust.

effects: Alignment shifts to neutral evil on failed DC 17 Charisma save
Book vanishes if no evil act performed in 10 days or a good act is done
Soul claimed by an evil entity if the wielder dies while attuned

entries.name: Eye and Hand of Vecna
entries: Wondrous item, artifact (requires attunement)
The Eye of Vecna and the Hand of Vecna might be found together or separately. The eye looks like a bloodshot organ torn free from the socket. The hand is a mummified and shriveled left extremity.

entries.name: Attunement Requirements
entries: To attune to the eye, you must gouge out your own eye and press the artifact into the empty socket. The eye grafts itself to your head and remains there until you die. Once in place, the eye transforms into a golden eye with a slit for a pupil, much like that of a cat. If the eye is ever removed, you die.

To attune to the hand, you must lop off your left hand at the wrist and press the artifact against the stump. The hand grafts itself to your arm and becomes a functioning appendage. If the hand is ever removed, you die.

entries.name: Properties of the Eye
entries: Your alignment changes to neutral evil, and you gain the following benefits:
You have truesight.
You can use an action to see as if you were wearing a ring of X-ray vision. You can end this effect as a bonus action.

The eye has 8 charges. You can use an action and expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18) from it: clairvoyance (2 charges), crown of madness (1 charge), disintegrate (4 charges), dominate monster (5 charges), or eyebite (4 charges). The eye regains 1d4 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. Each time you cast a spell from the eye, there is a 5 percent chance that Vecna tears your soul from your body, devours it, and then takes control of the body like a puppet. If that happens, you become an NPC under the DM's control.

entries.name: Properties of the Hand
entries: Your alignment changes to neutral evil, and you gain the following benefits:
Your Strength score becomes 20, unless it is already 20 or higher.
Any melee spell attack you make with the hand or any melee weapon attack made with a weapon held in the hand deals an extra 2d8 cold damage on a hit.

The hand has 8 charges. You can use an action and expend 1 or more charges to cast one of the following spells (save DC 18) from it: finger of death (5 charges), sleep (1 charge), slow (2 charges), or teleport (3 charges). The hand regains 1d4 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. Each time you cast a spell from the hand, it casts the suggestion spell on you (save DC 18), demanding that you commit an evil act. The act might have a specific intent or leave it up to you.

entries.name: Properties of the Eye and Hand (Combined)
entries: You are immune to disease and poison.
Using the eye's X-ray vision never causes you to gain exhaustion.
You experience premonitions of danger and, unless you are incapacitated, can't be surprised.
If you start your turn with at least 1 hit point, you regain 1d10 hit points.

If a creature has a skeleton, you can attempt to turn its bones to jelly with a touch of the Hand of Vecna. You can do so by using an action to make a melee attack against a creature you can reach, using your choice of your melee attack bonus for weapons or spells. On a hit, the target must succeed on a DC 18 Constitution saving throw or drop to 0 hit points.
You can use an action to cast wish. This property can be used again until 30 days have passed.

entries.name: Destroying the Eye and Hand
entries: If the Eye of Vecna and the Hand of Vecna are both attached to the same creature, and that creature is slain by the Sword of Kas, both the eye and the hand burst into flame, turn to ash, and are destroyed forever. Any other attempt to destroy them results in the artifacts reappearing in Vecna's vaults.

entries.name: Orb of Dragonkind
entries.description: An etched crystal globe about 10 inches in diameter. When used, it grows to about 20 inches in diameter, and mist swirls inside it. The orb contains the essence of an evil dragon, which resents attempts to coax magic from it. Those lacking in force of personality might find themselves enslaved to an orb.

properties.text: Forged by wizards of the Towers of High Sorcery on Krynn to aid in defeating evil dragons. Three are thought to survive, with their magic warped over time. The orb's dragon essence may desire annihilation of peoples, spread suffering, or advance worship of Takhisis (Tiamat's name on Krynn).

While attuned, use an action to speak the orb's command word and make a DC 15 Charisma check. On success, control the orb for as long as attuned. On failure, become charmed by the orb and cast suggestion (DC 18) at will, urging you to work toward the orb's evil ends.

Random Properties: 2 minor beneficial, 1 minor detrimental, 1 major detrimental. Spells: 7 charges (regain 1d4 + 3 daily at dawn). Cast cure wounds (3 charges), daylight (1), death ward (2), or scrying (3) with an action. Cast detect magic without charges.
Call Dragons: Use an action to issue a telepathic call (40-mile radius). Evil dragons must come to the orb as soon as possible. Cannot use again for 1 hour. Dragon deities like Tiamat are unaffected.
Destroying an Orb: Impervious to most damage. Disintegrate spell or a +3 magic weapon strike destroys it.

entries.name: Sword of Kas
entries.lore: Crafted by Vecna as a reward for his lieutenant Kas the Bloody Handed, this sentient longsword embodies chaos and destruction. Kas's rebellion against Vecna led to his demise, leaving only the sword as a remnant of his legacy. The weapon seeks to eradicate Vecna's influence and destroy those corrupted by the Eye and Hand of Vecna.

rules.name: Attack and Damage
rules.description: Grants a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it. Scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20, and deals an extra {@damage 2d10} slashing damage to undead.

rules.name: Blood Requirement
rules.description: If not bathed in blood within 1 minute of being drawn, the wielder must make a {@dc 15} Charisma saving throw. Success: takes {@damage 3d6} psychic damage. Failure: is dominated by the sword (as *dominate monster*) and must bathe it in blood.

rules.name: Spirit of Kas
rules.description: Adds a d10 to initiative at the start of every combat. When attacking with the sword, you can transfer some or all of its attack bonus to your Armor Class until the start of your next turn.

rules.name: Spells
rules.description: Can cast *call lightning*, *divine word*, or *finger of death* (save {@dc 18}) as an action. Once used, the spell cannot be cast again from the sword until the next dawn.

rules.name: Sentience
rules.description: A sentient chaotic weapon with Intelligence 15, Wisdom 13, Charisma 16. Has darkvision 60 ft. Communicates telepathically with its wielder and understands Common.

rules.name: Destroying the Sword
rules.description: A creature attuned to both the Eye of Vecna and the Hand of Vecna can use the *wish* spell to destroy it. The wielder must cast *wish* and make a Charisma check contested by the sword's Charisma check. The sword must be within 30 ft. Success: sword is destroyed; failure: nothing happens.

entries.name: Wand of Orcus
entries: The Wand of Orcus is an artifact requiring attunement. It is a sentient, chaotic evil item with an Intelligence of 16, Wisdom of 12, and Charisma of 16. It communicates telepathically with its wielder and understands Abyssal and Common.

entries.name: Attunement Requirements
items: Any creature besides Orcus must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw to attune.
On a successful save, the creature takes 10d6 necrotic damage.
On a failed save, the creature dies and rises as a zombie.

entries.name: Wielded Properties
entries: Functions as a magic mace with +3 to attack and damage rolls.
Deals an extra 2d12 necrotic damage on a hit.
Provides a +3 bonus to Armor Class while held.

entries.name: Spells
entries: Has 7 charges. Casts the following spells (save DC 18):
Regains 1d4 + 3 charges daily at dawn.
Orcus or his followers can cast spells with 2 fewer charges (minimum 0).

entries.name: Call Undead
entries: Conjures skeletons and zombies, dividing 500 HP among them (each undead has average HP).
Undead obey commands until destroyed or dawn, then collapse into inanimate piles.
Orcus can summon any undead, which persist until dismissed.

entries.name: Random Properties
entries: 2 minor beneficial properties
1 major beneficial property
2 minor detrimental properties
1 major detrimental property
Detrimental properties are suppressed while attuned to Orcus.

entries.name: Destruction
entries: Requires taking it to the Positive Energy Plane by the restored hero whose skull it bears.
Bathing in positive energy causes it to crack and explode unless the hero is restored.
Reforms on Orcus's layer of the Abyss if conditions are unmet.

entries.name: Blessing of Health
entries.description: Your Constitution score increases by 2, up to a maximum of 22.

entries.name: Blessing of Protection
entries.description: You gain a +1 bonus to AC and saving throws.

entries.name: Blessing of Magic Resistance
entries.description: You have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

entries.name: Blessing of Understanding
entries.description: Your Wisdom score increases by 2, up to a maximum of 22.

entries.name: Blessing of Valhalla
entries.description: This blessing grants you the power to summon spirit warriors, as if you had blown a silver horn of Valhalla. Once you use this blessing, you can't use it again until 7 days have passed.

entries.name: Blessing of Weapon Enhancement
entries.description: One nonmagical weapon in your possession becomes a +1 weapon whenever you wield it.

entries.name: Blessing of Wound Closure
entries.description: This blessing grants you the benefits of a periapt of wound closure.

entries.name: Charm of Animal Conjuring
entries.description: This charm allows you to cast the conjure animals spell (3rd-level version as an action. Once used three times, the charm vanishes from you.
effects: Casts conjure animals (3rd-level) as an action
Vanishes after three uses

entries.name: Charm of Darkvision
entries.description: This charm allows you to cast the darkvision spell as an action, no components required. Once used three times, the charm goes away.
effects: Casts darkvision as an action (no components)
Vanishes after three uses

entries.name: Charm of Feather Falling
entries.description: This charm grants you the benefits of a ring of feather falling. These benefits last for 10 days, after which the charm vanishes from you.
effects: Grants feather falling benefits for 10 days
Vanishes after duration expires

entries.name: Charm of Heroism
entries.description: This charm allows you to give yourself the benefit of a potion of heroism as an action. Once you do so, the charm vanishes from you.
effects: Grants potion of heroism benefit as an action
Vanishes after use

entries.name: Charm of Restoration
entries.description: This charm has 6 charges. You can use an action to expend some of its charges to cast one of the following spells: greater restoration (4 charges) or lesser restoration (2 charges). Once all its charges have been expended, the charm vanishes from you.
effects: 6 charges; use action to cast greater restoration (4 charges) or lesser restoration (2 charges)
Vanishes when charges are exhausted

entries.name: Charm of the Slayer
entries.description: One sword in your possession becomes a dragon slayer or giant slayer (DM's choice) for the next 9 days. The charm then vanishes from you and the weapon returns to normal.
effects: Transforms one weapon into a dragon/giant slayer for 9 days
Vanishes and weapon reverts to normal after duration

entries.name: Charm of Vitality
entries.description: This charm allows you to give yourself the benefit of a potion of vitality as an action. Once you do so, the charm vanishes from you.
effects: Grants potion of vitality benefit as an action
Vanishes after use

entries.name: Marks of Prestige
entries: Sometimes the most memorable reward for adventure is the prestige that they acquire throughout a realm. Their adventures often earn them fame and power, allies and enemies, and titles that they can pass on to their descendants. Some lords and ladies began as commoners who ventured into the dangerous places of the world and made names for themselves through the brave deeds.
This section details the most common marks of prestige that adventurers might acquire during a campaign. These marks are usually gained along with treasure, but sometimes they stand on their own.

entries.name: Letters of Recommendation
entries.description: A letter of recommendation from a person of impeccable reputation grants adventurers access to NPCs they would otherwise struggle to meet, such as nobles or high-ranking officials. It can also help resolve disputes with local authorities by lending credibility to the adventurers' claims. The letter is typically stored in a scroll tube, folio, or case for safekeeping.
entries.lore: The value of a letter of recommendation depends entirely on the reputation and influence of its writer. In regions where the writer holds no authority, the letter offers no tangible benefit. Adventurers often receive such letters as an alternative to monetary payment when gold is scarce.
entries.usage: Present the letter to NPCs or authorities to gain access to restricted individuals or situations. Its effectiveness hinges on the writer's standing in the area where it is presented.

entries.name: MEDALS
entries.description: Medals are symbolic awards given by political figures for acts of heroism, often crafted from precious materials. They hold significant cultural and social weight within specific regions or factions.
entries.lore: Medals influence NPC interactions based on their symbolic value. For example, the Golden Bear of Breland grants respect within Breland when displayed, while other medals may carry weight among allies of the awarding authority.

variants.name: Royal Badge of Valor
variants.description: A shield-shaped medal made of ruby and electrum, awarded for defending Breish citizens.
variants.lore: Symbolizes loyalty and heroism in Breland, granting respect from those who recognize its significance.

variants.name: Golden Bear of Breland
variants.description: A gold medal shaped like a bear's head with gemstone eyes, awarded for proving allegiance to the Brelish Crown by thwarting plots against the Treaty of Thronehold.
variants.lore: Conveys status as a hero of the people within Breland and carries weight among Breland's allies.

entries.name: Parcel of Land
entries.description: A parcel of land is granted by a royal letter as a reward for service. It is leased to a character and may include farms or villages, making them lord/lady of the land. Ownership remains with the local ruler, who can reclaim it if loyalty is questioned. Recipients may build on it, inherit it, but cannot sell/trade without permission. Ideal for adventurers seeking to settle or invest in a region.

entries.name: Special Favors
entries.description: A reward might come in the form of a favor that the characters can call on at some future date. Special favors work best when the individual granting them is trustworthy. A lawful good or lawful neutral NPC will do whatever can be done to fulfill an obligation when the time comes, short of breaking laws. A lawful evil NPC does the same, but only because a deal is a deal. A neutral good or neutral PC might pay off favors to protect his or her reputation. A chaotic good NPC is more concerned about doing right by the adventurers, honoring any obligations without worrying too much about personal risk or adherence to the law.

entries.name: Special Rights
entries.description: A politically powerful person can reward characters by granting them special rights, typically formalized in official documents. These rights may include carrying weapons in public, killing enemies of the crown, negotiating on a duke's behalf, demanding free lodging in communities, or commanding local militias. Special rights are temporary and can be revoked if abused.

entries.name: Training
